Worker state registry — shared JSON file tracking all active workers.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! The registry is informational: \"who is running, what are they doing, how\n4\t//! many beads processed.\" It is NOT used for coordination — heartbeats handle\n5\t//! that.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! File: `~/.needle/state/workers.json`\n8\t//! Access: flock-protected read-modify-write (atomic updates).\n9\t//!\n10\t//! serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n18\t\n19\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n20\t// PID liveness checking (platform-specific)\n21\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n22\t\n23\t/// Check if a process with the given PID is currently running.\n24\t///\n25\t/// Returns `false` if the PID does not exist or if we lack permission to signal it.\n26\t/// This is best-effort: if we can't determine liveness, we assume the process is dead\n27\t/// to avoid counting stale entries toward concurrency limits.\n28\tpub fn is_pid_alive(pid: u32) -> bool {\n29\t    #[cfg(unix)]\n30\t    {\n31\t        // Use libc::kill with signal 0 to check if process exists.\n32\t        // SAFETY: kill(pid, 0) only checks existence, doesn't send a signal.\n33\t        unsafe {\n34\t            let ret = libc::kill(pid as i32, 0);\n35\t            if ret == 0 {\n36\t                // kill succeeded: process exists and we have permission to signal it.\n37\t                return true;\n38\t            }\n39\t            // kill failed: check errno to distinguish EPERM from ESRCH.\n40\t            // We must read errno immediately after kill, before any other syscalls.\n41\t            let errno = *libc::__errno_location();\n42\t            match errno {\n43\t                libc::ESRCH => {\n44\t                    // No such process: PID is dead.\n45\t                    false\n46\t                }\n47\t                libc::EPERM => {\n48\t                    // Process exists but we don't have permission to signal it.\n49\t                    // We treat this as \"alive\" since the process actually exists.\n50\t                    true\n51\t                }\n52\t                _ => {\n53\t                    // Other errors (e.g., EINVAL for invalid signal).\n54\t                    // Conservatively treat as dead to avoid false positives.\n55\t                    false\n56\t                }\n57\t            }\n58\t        }\n59\t    }\n60\t\n61\t    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n62\t    {\n63\t        // On non-Unix platforms, conservatively return false (assume dead).\n64\t        // This prevents false positives where dead PIDs are counted as live.\n65\t        // TODO: Implement Windows liveness check via OpenProcess if needed.\n66\t        false\n67\t    }\n68\t}\n69\t\n70\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n71\t// WorkerEntry\n72\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n73\t\n74\t/// A single worker entry in the registry.\n75\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]\n76\tpub struct WorkerEntry {\n77\t    /// Fully-qualified worker identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`, e.g., `claude-foxtrot`).\n78\t    pub id: String,\n79\t    /// Process ID of the worker.\n80\t    pub pid: u32,\n81\t    /// Workspace the worker is processing beads from.\n82\t    pub workspace: PathBuf,\n83\t    /// Agent adapter name.\n84\t    pub agent: String,\n85\t    /// Model name (if known).\n86\t    pub model: Option<String>,\n87\t    /// Provider name (e.g., `anthropic`, `openai`).\n88\t    pub provider: Option<String>,\n89\t    /// When the worker started.\n90\t    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n91\t    /// Number of beads processed so far.\n92\t    pub beads_processed: u64,\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n96\t// RegistryFile\n97\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n98\t\n99\t/// The on-disk JSON structure for `workers.json`.\n100\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n101\tpub struct RegistryFile {\n102\t    pub workers: Vec<WorkerEntry>,\n103\t    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n104\t}\n105\t\n106\timpl Default for RegistryFile {\n107\t    fn default() -> Self {\n108\t        RegistryFile {\n109\t            workers: Vec::new(),\n110\t            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n111\t        }\n112\t    }\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n116\t// Registry\n117\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n118\t\n119\t/// Worker state registry with flock-protected read-modify-write access.\n120\tpub struct Registry {\n121\t    path: PathBuf,\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\timpl Registry {\n125\t    /// Create a registry instance pointing at the given state directory.\n126\t    ///\n127\t    /// The directory will be created on first write if it does not exist.\n128\t    pub fn new(state_dir: &Path) -> Self {\n129\t        Registry {\n130\t            path: state_dir.join(\"workers.json\"),\n131\t        }\n132\t    }\n133\t\n134\t    /// Create a registry using the default state directory (`~/.needle/state`).\n135\t    pub fn default_location(needle_home: &Path) -> Self {\n136\t        Self::new(&needle_home.join(\"state\"))\n137\t    }\n138\t\n139\t    /// Path to the workers.json file.\n140\t    pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {\n141\t        &self.path\n142\t    }\n143\t\n144\t    /// Register a worker on startup.\n145\t    ///\n146\t    /// If a worker with the same ID already exists, it is replaced (handles\n147\t    /// stale entries from previous runs).\n148\t    pub fn register(&self, entry: WorkerEntry) -> Result<()> {\n149\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n150\t            // Remove any existing entry with the same ID (stale from previous run).\n151\t            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != entry.id);\n152\t            reg.workers.push(entry);\n153\t        })\n154\t    }\n155\t\n156\t    /// Deregister a worker on shutdown.\n157\t    ///\n158\t    /// Best-effort: errors are logged but not propagated to avoid masking\n159\t    /// the real shutdown reason.\n160\t    pub fn deregister(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n161\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n162\t            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != worker_id);\n163\t        })\n164\t    }\n165\t\n166\t    /// Update a worker's beads_processed count.\n167\t    pub fn update_beads_processed(&self, worker_id: &str, beads_processed: u64) -> Result<()> {\n168\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n169\t            if let Some(entry) = reg.workers.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == worker_id) {\n170\t                entry.beads_processed = beads_processed;\n171\t            }\n172\t        })\n173\t    }\n174\t\n175\t    /// Read all registered workers, filtering out entries for dead PIDs.\n176\t    ///\n177\t    /// This lazy cleanup ensures that workers killed via SIGKILL or crashes\n178\t    /// don't accumulate in the registry and falsely count toward concurrency\n179\t    /// limits.\n180\t    pub fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<WorkerEntry>> {\n181\t        let reg = self.read()?;\n182\t        let total_count = reg.workers.len();\n183\t        let live_workers: Vec<WorkerEntry> = reg\n184\t            .workers\n185\t            .into_iter()\n186\t            .filter(|w| is_pid_alive(w.pid))\n187\t            .collect();\n188\t\n189\t        // If we filtered out dead entries, persist the cleanup so we don't\n190\t        // need to re-check their PIDs on every read. This is best-effort:\n191\t        // if the write fails (disk full, race with another writer), we still\n192\t        // return the correctly filtered list — we'll just re-filter next time.\n193\t        let live_count = live_workers.len();\n194\t        if live_count != total_count {\n195\t            let dead_count = total_count - live_count;\n196\t            tracing::debug!(dead_count, \"filtered dead worker entries from registry\");\n197\t\n198\t            let cleaned_reg = RegistryFile {\n199\t                workers: live_workers.clone(),\n200\t                updated_at: Utc::now(),\n201\t            };\n202\t            if let Err(e) = self.write_cleaned(cleaned_reg) {\n203\t                tracing::warn!(\n204\t                    error = %e,\n205\t                    \"failed to persist dead PID cleanup; will re-filter next read\"\n206\t                );\n207\t            }\n208\t        }\n209\t\n210\t        Ok(live_workers)\n211\t    }\n212\t\n213\t    /// Write a cleaned registry file (used by list() to persist dead PID filtering).\n214\t    ///\n215\t    /// No file locking: read() already released its shared lock before returning.\n216\t    /// The atomic rename ensures concurrent writers don't corrupt the file —\n217\t    /// one write wins cleanly.\n218\t    fn write_cleaned(&self, reg: RegistryFile) -> Result<()> {\n219\t        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n220\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n221\t        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n222\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n223\t        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n224\t            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n225\t        })?;\n226\t        Ok(())\n227\t    }\n228\t\n229\t    // ── Internal helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n230\t\n231\t    /// Read the registry file, returning a default if it doesn't exist.\n232\t    fn read(&self) -> Result<RegistryFile> {\n233\t        if !self.path.exists() {\n234\t            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n235\t        }\n236\t\n237\t        let file = std::fs::File::open(&self.path)\n238\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n239\t\n240\t        // Shared lock for reading (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n241\t        FileExt::lock_shared(&file)\n242\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to acquire shared lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n243\t\n244\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path)\n245\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n246\t\n247\t        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n248\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n249\t\n250\t        if content.trim().is_empty() {\n251\t            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n252\t        }\n253\t\n254\t        serde_json::from_str(&content)\n255\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to parse registry JSON: {}\", self.path.display()))\n256\t    }\n257\t\n258\t    /// Perform a flock-protected read-modify-write operation.\n259\t    fn modify<F>(&self, mutator: F) -> Result<()>\n260\t    where\n261\t        F: FnOnce(&mut RegistryFile),\n262\t    {\n263\t        // Ensure the parent directory exists.\n264\t        if let Some(parent) = self.path.parent() {\n265\t            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).with_context(|| {\n266\t                format!(\"failed to create registry directory: {}\", parent.display())\n267\t            })?;\n268\t        }\n269\t\n270\t        // Open or create the file.\n271\t        let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n272\t            .read(true)\n273\t            .write(true)\n274\t            .create(true)\n275\t            .truncate(false)\n276\t            .open(&self.path)\n277\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n278\t\n279\t        // Exclusive lock for writing (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n280\t        FileExt::lock_exclusive(&file).with_context(|| {\n281\t            format!(\"failed to acquire exclusive lock: {}\", self.path.display())\n282\t        })?;\n283\t\n284\t        // Read current contents.\n285\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path).unwrap_or_default();\n286\t        let mut reg: RegistryFile = if content.trim().is_empty() {\n287\t            RegistryFile::default()\n288\t        } else {\n289\t            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_default()\n290\t        };\n291\t\n292\t        // Apply the mutation.\n293\t        mutator(&mut reg);\n294\t        reg.updated_at = Utc::now();\n295\t\n296\t        // Write back atomically: write to temp file, then rename.\n297\t        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n298\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n299\t        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n300\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n301\t        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n302\t            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n303\t        })?;\n304\t\n305\t        // Release lock (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n306\t        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n307\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n308\t\n309\t        Ok(())\n310\t    }\n311\t}\n312\t\n313\t//   fn is_pid_alive_returns_true_for_current_process() {\n543\t        // The current process's PID should be alive.\n544\t        assert!(is_pid_alive(std::process::id()));\n545\t    }\n546\t\n547\t }\n553\t}\n554\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8d2ee6f9-9054-4e1b-b4ba-04f7ef472c61","uuid":"eb2f7659-b793-42b5-a8a7-d7eb262a3221","timestamp":"2026-04-24T12:31:52.298Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/registry/mod.rs","content":"//! Worker state registry — shared JSON file tracking all active workers.\n//!\n//! The registry is informational: \"who is running, what are they doing, how\n//! many beads processed.\" It is NOT used for coordination — heartbeats handle\n//! that.\n//!\n//! File: `~/.needle/state/workers.json`\n//! Access: flock-protected read-modify-write (atomic updates).\n//!\n//! Depends on: `config`, `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse fs2::FileExt;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PID liveness checking (platform-specific)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Check if a process with the given PID is currently running.\n///\n/// Returns `false` if the PID does not exist or if we lack permission to signal it.\n/// This is best-effort: if we can't determine liveness, we assume the process is dead\n/// to avoid counting stale entries toward concurrency limits.\npub fn is_pid_alive(pid: u32) -> bool {\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    {\n        // Use libc::kill with signal 0 to check if process exists.\n        // SAFETY: kill(pid, 0) only checks existence, doesn't send a signal.\n        unsafe {\n            let ret = libc::kill(pid as i32, 0);\n            if ret == 0 {\n                // kill succeeded: process exists and we have permission to signal it.\n                return true;\n            }\n            // kill failed: check errno to distinguish EPERM from ESRCH.\n            // We must read errno immediately after kill, before any other syscalls.\n            let errno = *libc::__errno_location();\n            match errno {\n                libc::ESRCH => {\n                    // No such process: PID is dead.\n                    false\n                }\n                libc::EPERM => {\n                    // Process exists but we don't have permission to signal it.\n                    // We treat this as \"alive\" since the process actually exists.\n                    true\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    // Other errors (e.g., EINVAL for invalid signal).\n                    // Conservatively treat as dead to avoid false positives.\n                    false\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    {\n        // On non-Unix platforms, conservatively return false (assume dead).\n        // This prevents false positives where dead PIDs are counted as live.\n        // TODO: Implement Windows liveness check via OpenProcess if needed.\n        false\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// WorkerEntry\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A single worker entry in the registry.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct WorkerEntry {\n    /// Fully-qualified worker identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`, e.g., `claude-foxtrot`).\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Process ID of the worker.\n    pub pid: u32,\n    /// Workspace the worker is processing beads from.\n    pub workspace: PathBuf,\n    /// Agent adapter name.\n    pub agent: String,\n    /// Model name (if known).\n    pub model: Option<String>,\n    /// Provider name (e.g., `anthropic`, `openai`).\n    pub provider: Option<String>,\n    /// When the worker started.\n    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Number of beads processed so far.\n    pub beads_processed: u64,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// RegistryFile\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// The on-disk JSON structure for `workers.json`.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RegistryFile {\n    pub workers: Vec<WorkerEntry>,\n    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for RegistryFile {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        RegistryFile {\n            workers: Vec::new(),\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Registry\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Worker state registry with flock-protected read-modify-write access.\npub struct Registry {\n    path: PathBuf,\n}\n\nimpl Registry {\n    /// Create a registry instance pointing at the given state directory.\n    ///\n    /// The directory will be created on first write if it does not exist.\n    pub fn new(state_dir: &Path) -> Self {\n        Registry {\n            path: state_dir.join(\"workers.json\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a registry using the default state directory (`~/.needle/state`).\n    pub fn default_location(needle_home: &Path) -> Self {\n        Self::new(&needle_home.join(\"state\"))\n    }\n\n    /// Path to the workers.json file.\n    pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {\n        &self.path\n    }\n\n    /// Register a worker on startup.\n    ///\n    /// If a worker with the same ID already exists, it is replaced (handles\n    /// stale entries from previous runs).\n    pub fn register(&self, entry: WorkerEntry) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            // Remove any existing entry with the same ID (stale from previous run).\n            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != entry.id);\n            reg.workers.push(entry);\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Deregister a worker on shutdown.\n    ///\n    /// Best-effort: errors are logged but not propagated to avoid masking\n    /// the real shutdown reason.\n    pub fn deregister(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != worker_id);\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Update a worker's beads_processed count.\n    pub fn update_beads_processed(&self, worker_id: &str, beads_processed: u64) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            if let Some(entry) = reg.workers.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == worker_id) {\n                entry.beads_processed = beads_processed;\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Read all registered workers, filtering out entries for dead PIDs.\n    ///\n    /// This lazy cleanup ensures that workers killed via SIGKILL or crashes\n    /// don't accumulate in the registry and falsely count toward concurrency\n    /// limits.\n    pub fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<WorkerEntry>> {\n        let reg = self.read()?;\n        let total_count = reg.workers.len();\n        let live_workers: Vec<WorkerEntry> = reg\n            .workers\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter(|w| is_pid_alive(w.pid))\n            .collect();\n\n        // If we filtered out dead entries, persist the cleanup so we don't\n        // need to re-check their PIDs on every read. This is best-effort:\n        // if the write fails (disk full, race with another writer), we still\n        // return the correctly filtered list — we'll just re-filter next time.\n        let live_count = live_workers.len();\n        if live_count != total_count {\n            let dead_count = total_count - live_count;\n            tracing::debug!(dead_count, \"filtered dead worker entries from registry\");\n\n            let cleaned_reg = RegistryFile {\n                workers: live_workers.clone(),\n                updated_at: Utc::now(),\n            };\n            if let Err(e) = self.write_cleaned(cleaned_reg) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to persist dead PID cleanup; will re-filter next read\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(live_workers)\n    }\n\n    /// Write a cleaned registry file (used by list() to persist dead PID filtering).\n    ///\n    /// No file locking: read() already released its shared lock before returning.\n    /// The atomic rename ensures concurrent writers don't corrupt the file —\n    /// one write wins cleanly.\n    fn write_cleaned(&self, reg: RegistryFile) -> Result<()> {\n        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n        })?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Internal helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Read the registry file, returning a default if it doesn't exist.\n    fn read(&self) -> Result<RegistryFile> {\n        if !self.path.exists() {\n            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n        }\n\n        let file = std::fs::File::open(&self.path)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        // Shared lock for reading (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n        FileExt::lock_shared(&file)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to acquire shared lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        if content.trim().is_empty() {\n            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n        }\n\n        serde_json::from_str(&content)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to parse registry JSON: {}\", self.path.display()))\n    }\n\n    /// Perform a flock-protected read-modify-write operation.\n    fn modify<F>(&self, mutator: F) -> Result<()>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&mut RegistryFile),\n    {\n        // Ensure the parent directory exists.\n        if let Some(parent) = self.path.parent() {\n            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).with_context(|| {\n                format!(\"failed to create registry directory: {}\", parent.display())\n            })?;\n        }\n\n        // Open or create the file.\n        let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n            .read(true)\n            .write(true)\n            .create(true)\n            .truncate(false)\n            .open(&self.path)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        // Exclusive lock for writing (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n        FileExt::lock_exclusive(&file).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\"failed to acquire exclusive lock: {}\", self.path.display())\n        })?;\n\n        // Read current contents.\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path).unwrap_or_default();\n        let mut reg: RegistryFile = if content.trim().is_empty() {\n            RegistryFile::default()\n        } else {\n            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_default()\n        };\n\n        // Apply the mutation.\n        mutator(&mut reg);\n        reg.updated_at = Utc::now();\n\n        // Write back atomically: write to temp file, then rename.\n        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n        })?;\n\n        // Release lock (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n//   started_at: Utc::now(),\n            beads_processed: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// A PID that is definitely not in use on any real system.\n    ///\n    /// This must be a value that:\n    /// 1. assert_eq!(workers[1].id, \"bravo\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn deregister_removes_worker() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"bravo\")).unwrap();\n        reg.deregister(\"alpha\").unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].id, \"bravo\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn deregister_nonexistent_is_noop() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n Health monitoring: heartbeats, stale detection, PID checking.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Workers emit periodic heartbeats from a dedicated background thread.\n4\t//! Peers read heartbeat files to detect crashed or stuck workers.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! The heartbeat emitter uses `std::thread::spawn` (not tokio) to keep it\n7\t//! independent of the async runtime. The main worker updates shared state\n8\t//! via `Arc<Mutex<SharedHeartbeatState>>`.\n9\t//!\n10\t//! heartbeat_dir,\n98\t            heartbeat_interval,\n99\t            heartbeat_ttl,\n100\t            worker_id: worker_name,\n101\t            qualified_id,\n102\t            workspace:         emitter_handle: None,\n111\t        }\n112\t    }\n113\t\n114\t    /// Start the background heartbeat emitter thread.\n115\t    ///\n116\t    /// The thread writes a heartbeat JSON file every `heartbeat_interval` until\n117\t    /// `stop()` is called.\n118\t    pub fn start_emitter(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n119\t        // Ensure heartbeat directory exists.\n120\t        std::fs::create_dir_all(&self.heartbeat_dir).with_context(|| {\n121\t            format!(\n122\t                \"failed to create heartbeat directory: {}\",\n123\t                self.heartbeat_dir.display()\n124\t            )\n125\t        })?;\n126\t\n127\t        // Write the initial heartbeat immediately.\n128\t        self.write_heartbeat()?;\n129\t\n130\t        let self.worker_id.clone();\n134\t        let qualified_id = self.qualified_id.clone();\n135\t        let workspace = self.workspace.clone();\n136\t        let started_at = self.started_at;\n137\t || {\n142\t                emitter_loop(\n143\t                    shared_state,\n144\t                    shutdown,\n145\t                    heartbeat_dir,\n146\t              );\n153\t            })\n154\t            .context(\"failed to spawn heartbeat emitter thread\")?;\n155\t\n156\t        self.emitter_handle = Some(handle);\n157\t        tracing::info!(\n158\t            worker = %self.worker_id,\n159\t            interval_secs = self.heartbeat_interval.as_secs(),\n160\t            \"heartbeat emitter started\"\n161\t        );\n162\t\n163\t        Ok(())\n164\t    }\n165\t\n166\t    /// Update the worker state visible to the heartbeat emitter.\n167\t    ///\n168\t    /// Called by the worker on every state transition.\n169\t    pub fn update_state(&self, state: &WorkerState, current_bead: Option<&BeadId>) {\n170\t        if let Ok(mut guard) = self.shared_state.lock() {\n171\t            guard.state = state.clone();\n172\t            guard.current_bead = current_bead.cloned();\n173\t        }\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    /// Update the beads_processed count visible to the heartbeat emitter.\n177\t    pub fn update_beads_processed(&self, count: u64) {\n178\t        if let Ok(mut guard) = self.shared_state.lock() {\n179\t            guard.beads_processed = count;\n180\t        }\n181\t    }\n182\t\n183\t    /// Stop the heartbeat emitter and remove this worker's heartbeat file.\n184\t    ///\n185\t    /// Called on graceful shutdown (STOPPED) and best-effort on ERRORED.\n186\t    pub fn stop(&mut self) {\n187\t        // Signal the emitter thread to exit.\n188\t        self.shutdown.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n189\t\n190\t        // Join the emitter thread (with a timeout to avoid hanging).\n191\t        if let Some(handle) = self.emitter_handle.take() {\n192\t            // Give the thread up to 2x the interval to notice shutdown and exit.\n193\t            let _ = handle.join();\n194\t        }\n195\t\n196\t        // Remove the heartbeat file (best-effort).\n197\t        let path = self.heartbeat_path();\n198\t        if path.exists() {\n199\t            if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n200\t                tracing::warn!(\n201\t                    error = %e,\n202\t                    path = %path.display(),\n203\t                    \"failed to remove heartbeat file on shutdown\"\n204\t                );\n205\t            } else {\n206\t                tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"heartbeat file removed\");\n207\t            }\n208\t        }\n209\t    }\n210\t\n211\t    /// Path to this worker's heartbeat file.\n212\t    ///\n213\t    /// Keyed by fully-qualified identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`) to prevent\n214\t    /// collisions when workers from different adapter pools share a NATO name.\n215\t    pub fn heartbeat_path(&self) -> PathBuf {\n216\t        self.heartbeat_dir\n217\t            .join(format!(\"{}.json\", self.qualified_id))\n218\t    }\n219\t\n220\t    /// The fully-qualified identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`).\n221\t    pub fn qualified_id(&self) -> &str {\n222\t        &self.qualified_id\n223\t    }\n224\t\n225\t    /// Directory where heartbeat files are stored.\n226\t    pub fn heartbeat_dir(&self) -> &Path {\n227\t        &self.heartbeat_dir\n228\t    }\n229\t\n230\t    /// The configured heartbeat TTL.\n231\t    pub fn heartbeat_ttl(&self) -> Duration {\n232\t        self.heartbeat_ttl\n233\t    }\n234\t\n235\t    // ── Reader utilities (used by peer monitoring / Mend strand) ────────────\n236\t\n237\t    /// Read all heartbeat files in the given directory.\n238\t    ///\n239\t    /// Silently skips files that cannot be read or parsed (they may be\n240\t    /// partially written or from a crashed worker).\n241\t    pub fn read_all_heartbeats(dir: &Path) -> Result<Vec<HeartbeatData>> {\n242\t        let mut heartbeats = Vec::new();\n243\t\n244\t        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(dir) {\n245\t            Ok(e) => e,\n246\t            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(heartbeats),\n247\t            Err(e) => {\n248\t                return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(\n249\t                    \"failed to read heartbeat directory {}: {}\",\n250\t                    dir.display(),\n251\t                    e\n252\t                ));\n253\t            }\n254\t        };\n255\t\n256\t        for entry in entries {\n257\t            let entry = match entry {\n258\t                Ok(e) => e,\n259\t                Err(_) => continue,\n260\t            };\n261\t\n262\t            let path = entry.path();\n263\t            if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) != Some(\"json\") {\n264\t                continue;\n265\t            }\n266\t\n267\t            match std::fs::read_to_string(&path) {\n268\t                Ok(content) => match serde_json::from_str::<HeartbeatData>(&content) {\n269\t                    Ok(mut hb) => {\n270\t                        // Backfill qualified_id for heartbeats written by older versions.\n271\t                        if hb.qualified_id.is_empty() {\n272\t                            hb.qualified_id = hb.worker_id.clone();\n273\t                        }\n274\t                        hb.heartbeat_file = Some(path.clone());\n275\t                        heartbeats.push(hb)\n276\t                    }\n277\t                    Err(e) => {\n278\t                        tracing::debug!(\n279\t                            path = %path.display(),\n280\t                            error = %e,\n281\t                            \"skipping unparseable heartbeat file\"\n282\t                        );\n283\t                    }\n284\t                },\n285\t                Err(e) => {\n286\t                    tracing::debug!(\n287\t                        path = %path.display(),\n288\t                        error = %e,\n289\t                        \"skipping unreadable heartbeat file\"\n290\t                    );\n291\t                }\n292\t            }\n293\t        }\n294\t\n295\t        Ok(heartbeats)\n296\t    }\n297\t\n298\t    /// Check whether a heartbeat is stale (exceeded TTL).\n299\t    pub fn is_stale(heartbeat: &HeartbeatData, ttl: Duration) -> bool {\n300\t        let age = Utc::now()\n301\t            .signed_duration_since(heartbeat.last_heartbeat)\n302\t            .to_std()\n303\t            .unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO);\n304\t        age > ttl\n305\t    }\n306\t\n307\t    /// Check whether a process with the given PID is alive.\n308\t    ///\n309\t    /// Uses `kill -0` semantics: sends signal 0 to check existence without\n310\t    /// actually sending a signal.\n311\t    pub fn check_pid_alive(pid: u32) -> bool {\n312\t        std::process::Command::new(\"kill\")\n313\t            .args([\"-0\", &pid.to_string()])\n314\t            .stdout(std::process::Stdio::null())\n315\t            .stderr(std::process::Stdio::null())\n316\t            .status()\n317\t            .map(|s| s.success())\n318\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n319\t    }\n320\t\n321\t    /// Detect peers with stale heartbeats.\n322\t    ///\n323\t    /// Returns a list of stale peers, excluding this worker.\n324\t    pub fn detect_stale_peers(&self) -> Result<Vec<StalePeer>> {\n325\t        let heartbeats = Self::read_all_heartbeats(&self.heartbeat_dir)?;\n326\t        let mut stale = Vec::new();\n327\t\n328\t        for hb in heartbeats {\n329\t            // Skip our own heartbeat.\n330\t            if hb.qualified_id == self.qualified_id {\n331\t                continue;\n332\t            }\n333\t\n334\t            if Self::is_stale(&hb, self.heartbeat_ttl) {\n335\t                let pid_alive = Self::check_pid_alive(hb.pid);\n336\t                let hb_file = hb.heartbeat_file.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n337\t                    self.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", hb.qualified_id))\n338\t                });\n339\t                stale.push(StalePeer {\n340\t                    worker_id: hb.worker_id.clone(),\n341\t                    qualified_id: Some(hb.qualified_id.clone()),\n342\t                    pid: hb.pid,\n343\t                    pid_alive,\n344\t                    current_bead: hb.current_bead.clone(),\n345\t                    last_heartbeat: hb.last_heartbeat,\n346\t                    heartbeat_file: hb_file,\n347\t                });\n348\t            }\n349\t        }\n350\t\n351\t        Ok(stale)\n352\t    }\n353\t\n354\t    // ── Internal ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n355\t\n356\t    /// Write a heartbeat file atomically (write temp, then rename).\n357\t    fn write_heartbeat(&self) -> Result<()> {\n358\t        let (state, current_bead, beads_processed) = {\n359\t            let guard = self\n360\t                .shared_state\n361\t                .lock()\n362\t                .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"shared state lock poisoned: {e}\"))?;\n363\t            (\n364\t                guard.state.clone(),\n365\t                guard.current_bead.clone(),\n366\t                guard.beads_processed,\n367\t            )\n368\t        };\n369\t\n370\t        let data = HeartbeatData {\n371\t            worker_id: self.worker_id.clone(),\n372\t            qualified_id: self.qualified_id.clone(),\n373\t            pid: std::process::id(),\n374\t            state,\n375\t            current_bead,\n376\t            workspace: self.workspace.clone(),\n377\t            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n378\t            started_at: self.started_at,\n379\t            beads_processed,\n380\t            session: self.worker_id.clone(),\n381\t            heartbeat_file: None,\n382\t        };\n383\t\n384\t        let path = self.heartbeat_path();\n385\t        let tmp_path = path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n386\t\n387\t        // Auto-create parent directory so that heartbeats self-recover if the\n388\t        // directory is deleted while a worker is running.\n389\t        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n390\t            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)\n391\t                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to create heartbeat dir: {}\", parent.display()))?;\n392\t        }\n393\t\n394\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&data).context(\"failed to serialize heartbeat\")?;\n395\t        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, json.as_bytes()).with_context(|| {\n396\t            format!(\n397\t                \"failed to write temp heartbeat file: {}\",\n398\t                tmp_path.display()\n399\t            )\n400\t        })?;\n401\t        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path).with_context(|| {\n402\t            format!(\n403\t                \"failed to rename heartbeat file: {} -> {}\",\n404\t                tmp_path.display(),\n405\t                path.display()\n406\t            )\n407\t        })?;\n408\t\n409\t        Ok(())\n410\t    }\n411\t}\n412\t\n413\timpl Drop for HealthMonitor {\n414\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n415\t        // Best-effort: signal the emitter and clean up the heartbeat file.\n416\t        self.stop();\n417\t    }\n418\t}\n419\t\n420\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n421\t// StalePeer\n422\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n423\t\n424\t/// A peer worker detected as having a stale heartbeat.\n425\t#[derive(Debug)]\n426\tpub struct StalePeer {\n427\t    pub worker_id: String,\n428\t    /// Fully-qualified identity of the peer.\n429\t    pub qualified_id: Option<String>,\n430\t    pub pid: u32,\n431\t    pub pid_alive: bool,\n432\t    pub current_bead: Option<BeadId>,\n433\t    pub last_heartbeat: DateTime<Utc>,\n434\t    pub heartbeat_file: PathBuf,\n435\t}\n436\t\n437\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n438\t// Emitter loop (runs in a dedicated std::thread)\n439\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n440\t\n441\t/// Maximum sleep between heartbeat attempts when backing off on failure.\n442\tconst MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF: Duration = Duration::from_secs(5 * 60);\n443\t\n444\t/// Background emitter loop. Writes heartbeat at each interval.\n445\t///\n446\t/// Circuit breaker: after `max_consecutive_failures` consecutive write failures\n447\t/// the loop sets the shutdown flag and exits so the worker terminates instead of\n448\t/// spinning indefinitely.\n449\t///\n450\t/// Backoff: each consecutive failure doubles the inter-attempt sleep, capped at\n451\t/// [`MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF`].\n452\t#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n453\tfn emitter_loop(\n454\t    shared_state: Arc<Mutex<SharedHeartbeatState>>,\n455\t    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n456\t    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n457\t    worker_id: String,\n458\t    qualified_id: String,\n459\t    workspace: PathBuf,\n460\t    started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n461\t    interval: Duration,\n462\t    max_consecutive_failures: u32,\n463\t) {\n464\t    // Ensure the heartbeat directory exists before entering the write loop so\n465\t    // that workers self-recover if ~/.needle/state/heartbeats/ is deleted.\n466\t    if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(&heartbeat_dir) {\n467\t        tracing::error!(\n468\t            error = %e,\n469\t            dir = %heartbeat_dir.display(),\n470\t            \"failed to create heartbeat directory\"\n471\t        );\n472\t    }\n473\t\n474\t    let mut consecutive_failures: u32 = 0;\n475\t    let mut current_sleep = interval;\n476\t\n477\t    loop {\n478\t        std::thread::sleep(current_sleep);\n479\t\n480\t        if shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {\n481\t            tracing::debug!(worker = %worker_id, \"heartbeat emitter shutting down\");\n482\t            return;\n483\t        }\n484\t\n485\t        let (state, current_bead, beads_processed) = match shared_state.lock() {\n486\t            Ok(guard) => (\n487\t                guard.state.clone(),\n488\t                guard.current_bead.clone(),\n489\t                guard.beads_processed,\n490\t            ),\n491\t            Err(_) => {\n492\t                // Mutex poisoned — the main thread panicked. Exit.\n493\t                tracing::error!(\n494\t                    worker = %worker_id,\n495\t                    \"shared state mutex poisoned, heartbeat emitter exiting\"\n496\t                );\n497\t                return;\n498\t            }\n499\t        };\n500\t\n501\t        let data = HeartbeatData {\n502\t            worker_id: worker_id.clone(),\n503\t            qualified_id: qualified_id.clone(),\n504\t            pid: std::process::id(),\n505\t            state,\n506\t            current_bead,\n507\t            workspace: workspace.clone(),\n508\t            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n509\t            started_at,\n510\t            beads_processed,\n511\t            session: worker_id.clone(),\n512\t            heartbeat_file: None,\n513\t        };\n514\t\n515\t        let path = heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", qualified_id));\n516\t        let tmp_path = path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n517\t\n518\t        let write_result: anyhow::Result<()> = (|| {\n519\t            let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&data)?;\n520\t            std::fs::write(&tmp_path, json.as_bytes())?;\n521\t            std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path)?;\n522\t            Ok(())\n523\t        })();\n524\t\n525\t        match write_result {\n526\t            Ok(()) => {\n527\t                consecutive_failures = 0;\n528\t                current_sleep = interval;\n529\t            }\n530\t            Err(e) => {\n531\t                consecutive_failures += 1;\n532\t                tracing::error!(\n533\t                    error = %e,\n534\t                    worker = %worker_id,\n535\t                    consecutive_failures,\n536\t                    max = max_consecutive_failures,\n537\t                    \"heartbeat write failed\"\n538\t                );\n539\t                if consecutive_failures >= max_consecutive_failures {\n540\t                    tracing::error!(\n541\t                        worker = %worker_id,\n542\t                        consecutive_failures,\n543\t                        \"heartbeat emitter circuit breaker triggered — worker will shut down\"\n544\t                    );\n545\t                    shutdown.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n546\t                    return;\n547\t                }\n548\t                // Exponential backoff to reduce log spam before the circuit breaker fires.\n549\t                current_sleep = current_sleep.saturating_mul(2).min(MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF);\n550\t            }\n551\t        }\n552\t    }\n553\t}\n554\t\n555\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n556\t// Tests\n557\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n558\t\n559\t#[cfg(test)]\n560\tmod tests {\n561\t    use super::*;\n562\t    use std::time::Duration;\n563\t\n564\t consecutive write failures:\n841\t    /// the emitter must set the shutdown flag and return rather than looping forever.\n842\t    #[test]\n843\t    fn emitter_exits_after_consecutive_failures() {\n844\t        use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;\n845\t\n846\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n847\t        let hb_dir = dir.path().join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n848\t        std::fs::create_dir_all(&hb_dir).unwrap();\n849\t\n850\t        // Make the heartbeat directory read-only so every write attempt fails.\n851\t        std::fs::set_permissions(&hb_dir, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o555)).unwrap();\n852\t\n853\t        let shared_state = Arc::new(Mutex::new(SharedHeartbeatState {\n854\t            state: WorkerState::Selecting,\n855\t            current_bead: None,\n856\t            beads_processed: 0,\n857\t        }));\n858\t        let shutdown = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));\n859\t\n860\t        let shutdown_clone = shutdown.clone();\n861\t        let shared_state_clone = shared_state.clone();\n862\t        let hb_dir_clone = hb_dir.clone();\n863\t\n864\t        // Use a tiny interval and a low failure threshold so the test completes quickly.\n865\t        let handle = std::thread::spawn(move || {\n866\t            emitter_loop(\n867\t                shared_state_clone,\n868\t                shutdown_clone,\n869\t                hb_dir_clone,\n870\t                \"cb-test\".to_string(),\n871\t                \"claude-cb-test\".to_string(),\n872\t                PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n873\t                Utc::now(),\n874\t                Duration::from_millis(1),\n875\t                3, // trip after 3 consecutive failures\n876\t            );\n877\t        });\n878\t\n879\t        handle.join().expect(\"emitter thread panicked\");\n880\t\n881\t        // The circuit breaker must have set the shutdown flag.\n882\t        assert!(\n883\t            shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst),\n884\t            \"shutdown flag must be set after circuit breaker trips\"\n885\t        );\n886\t\n887\t        // Restore permissions so the tempdir can be cleaned up.\n888\t        let _ = std::fs::set_permissions(&hb_dir, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o755));\n889\t    }\n890\t}\n891\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8d2ee6f9-9054-4e1b-b4ba-04f7ef472c61","uuid":"bd717813-638b-411a-b020-4095b503c88b","timestamp":"2026-04-24T12:32:08.944Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/health/mod.rs","content":"//! Health monitoring: heartbeats, stale detection, PID checking.\n//!\n//! Workers emit periodic heartbeats from a dedicated background thread.\n//! Peers read heartbeat files to detect crashed or stuck workers.\n//!\n//! The heartbeat emitter uses `std::thread::spawn` (not tokio) to keep it\n//! independent of the async runtime. The main worker updates shared state\n//! via `Arc<Mutex<SharedHeartbeatState>>`.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `config`, `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\nuse crate::config::Config;\nuse crate::telemetry::Telemetry;\nuse crate::types::{BeadId, WorkerState};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// HeartbeatData — on-disk JSON structure\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Data written to the heartbeat JSON file on disk.\n///\n/// Path: `~/.needle/state/heartbeats/<qualified-id>.json`\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct HeartbeatData {\n    /// Bare NATO name (e.g., \"alpha\", \"foxtrot\").\n    pub worker_id: String,\n    /// Fully-qualified identity: `{adapter}-{worker_id}` (e.g., \"claude-code-glm-5-foxtrot\").\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub qualified_id: String,\n    pub pid: u32,\n    pub state: WorkerState,\n    pub current_bead: Option<BeadId>,\n    pub workspace: PathBuf,\n    pub last_heartbeat: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub beads_processed: u64,\n    pub session: String,\n    /// The filename that produced this heartbeat (set during read, not serialized).\n    #[serde(skip)]\n    pub heartbeat_file: Option<PathBuf>,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// SharedHeartbeatState — updated by worker, read by emitter\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Shared state between the main worker loop and the heartbeat emitter thread.\nstruct SharedHeartbeatState {\n    state: WorkerState,\n    current_bead: Option<BeadId>,\n    beads_processed: u64,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// HealthMonitor\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Health monitor for a single worker.\n///\n/// Owns the background heartbeat emitter thread and provides reader utilities\n/// for peer heartbeat files.\npub struct HealthMonitor {\n    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n    heartbeat_interval: Duration,\n    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n    /// Bare NATO name (e.g., \"alpha\", \"foxtrot\").\n    worker_id: String,\n    /// Fully-qualified identity: `{adapter_slug}-{worker_id}` (e.g., \"claude-code-glm-5-foxtrot\").\n    qualified_id: String,\n    workspace: PathBuf,\n    started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    shared_state: Arc<Mutex<SharedHeartbeatState>>,\n    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    emitter_handle: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,\n}\n\nimpl HealthMonitor {\n    /// Create a new health monitor.\n    ///\n    /// Does not start the emitter — call `start_emitter()` after construction.\n    pub fn new(config: Config, worker_name: String, _telemetry: Telemetry) -> Self {\n        let heartbeat_dir = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let heartbeat_interval = Duration::from_secs(config.health.heartbeat_interval_secs);\n        let heartbeat_ttl = Duration::from_secs(config.health.heartbeat_ttl_secs);\n        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n\n        HealthMonitor {\n            heartbeat_dir,\n            heartbeat_interval,\n            heartbeat_ttl,\n            worker_id: worker_name,\n            qualified_id,\n            workspace: config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            started_at: Utc::now(),\n            shared_state: Arc::new(Mutex::new(SharedHeartbeatState {\n                state: WorkerState::Booting,\n                current_bead: None,\n                beads_processed: 0,\n            })),\n            shutdown: Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false)),\n            emitter_handle: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background heartbeat emitter thread.\n    ///\n    /// The thread writes a heartbeat JSON file every `heartbeat_interval` until\n    /// `stop()` is called.\n    pub fn start_emitter(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n        // Ensure heartbeat directory exists.\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&self.heartbeat_dir).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\n                \"failed to create heartbeat directory: {}\",\n                self.heartbeat_dir.display()\n            )\n        })?;\n\n        // Write the initial heartbeat immediately.\n        self.write_heartbeat()?;\n\n        let shared_state = self.shared_state.clone();\n        let shutdown = self.shutdown.clone();\n        let heartbeat_dir = self.heartbeat_dir.clone();\n        let worker_id = self.worker_id.clone();\n        let qualified_id = self.qualified_id.clone();\n        let workspace = self.workspace.clone();\n        let started_at = self.started_at;\n        let interval = self.heartbeat_interval;\n\n        let handle = std::thread::Builder::new()\n            .name(format!(\"heartbeat-{}\", self.worker_id))\n            .spawn(move || {\n                emitter_loop(\n                    shared_state,\n                    shutdown,\n                    heartbeat_dir,\n                    worker_id,\n                    qualified_id,\n                    workspace,\n                    started_at,\n                    interval,\n                    10,\n                );\n            })\n            .context(\"failed to spawn heartbeat emitter thread\")?;\n\n        self.emitter_handle = Some(handle);\n        tracing::info!(\n            worker = %self.worker_id,\n            interval_secs = self.heartbeat_interval.as_secs(),\n            \"heartbeat emitter started\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update the worker state visible to the heartbeat emitter.\n    ///\n    /// Called by the worker on every state transition.\n    pub fn update_state(&self, state: &WorkerState, current_bead: Option<&BeadId>) {\n        if let Ok(mut guard) = self.shared_state.lock() {\n            guard.state = state.clone();\n            guard.current_bead = current_bead.cloned();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update the beads_processed count visible to the heartbeat emitter.\n    pub fn update_beads_processed(&self, count: u64) {\n        if let Ok(mut guard) = self.shared_state.lock() {\n            guard.beads_processed = count;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Stop the heartbeat emitter and remove this worker's heartbeat file.\n    ///\n    /// Called on graceful shutdown (STOPPED) and best-effort on ERRORED.\n    pub fn stop(&mut self) {\n        // Signal the emitter thread to exit.\n        self.shutdown.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n\n        // Join the emitter thread (with a timeout to avoid hanging).\n        if let Some(handle) = self.emitter_handle.take() {\n            // Give the thread up to 2x the interval to notice shutdown and exit.\n            let _ = handle.join();\n        }\n\n        // Remove the heartbeat file (best-effort).\n        let path = self.heartbeat_path();\n        if path.exists() {\n            if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    path = %path.display(),\n                    \"failed to remove heartbeat file on shutdown\"\n                );\n            } else {\n                tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"heartbeat file removed\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Path to this worker's heartbeat file.\n    ///\n    /// Keyed by fully-qualified identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`) to prevent\n    /// collisions when workers from different adapter pools share a NATO name.\n    pub fn heartbeat_path(&self) -> PathBuf {\n        self.heartbeat_dir\n            .join(format!(\"{}.json\", self.qualified_id))\n    }\n\n    /// The fully-qualified identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`).\n    pub fn qualified_id(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.qualified_id\n    }\n\n    /// Directory where heartbeat files are stored.\n    pub fn heartbeat_dir(&self) -> &Path {\n        &self.heartbeat_dir\n    }\n\n    /// The configured heartbeat TTL.\n    pub fn heartbeat_ttl(&self) -> Duration {\n        self.heartbeat_ttl\n    }\n\n    // ── Reader utilities (used by peer monitoring / Mend strand) ────────────\n\n    /// Read all heartbeat files in the given directory.\n    ///\n    /// Silently skips files that cannot be read or parsed (they may be\n    /// partially written or from a crashed worker).\n    pub fn read_all_heartbeats(dir: &Path) -> Result<Vec<HeartbeatData>> {\n        let mut heartbeats = Vec::new();\n\n        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(dir) {\n            Ok(e) => e,\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(heartbeats),\n            Err(e) => {\n                return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(\n                    \"failed to read heartbeat directory {}: {}\",\n                    dir.display(),\n                    e\n                ));\n            }\n        };\n\n        for entry in entries {\n            let entry = match entry {\n                Ok(e) => e,\n                Err(_) => continue,\n            };\n\n            let path = entry.path();\n            if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) != Some(\"json\") {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            match std::fs::read_to_string(&path) {\n                Ok(content) => match serde_json::from_str::<HeartbeatData>(&content) {\n                    Ok(mut hb) => {\n                        // Backfill qualified_id for heartbeats written by older versions.\n                        if hb.qualified_id.is_empty() {\n                            hb.qualified_id = hb.worker_id.clone();\n                        }\n                        hb.heartbeat_file = Some(path.clone());\n                        heartbeats.push(hb)\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        tracing::debug!(\n                            path = %path.display(),\n                            error = %e,\n                            \"skipping unparseable heartbeat file\"\n                        );\n                    }\n                },\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::debug!(\n                        path = %path.display(),\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"skipping unreadable heartbeat file\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(heartbeats)\n    }\n\n    /// Check whether a heartbeat is stale (exceeded TTL).\n    pub fn is_stale(heartbeat: &HeartbeatData, ttl: Duration) -> bool {\n        let age = Utc::now()\n            .signed_duration_since(heartbeat.last_heartbeat)\n            .to_std()\n            .unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO);\n        age > ttl\n    }\n\n    /// Check whether a process with the given PID is alive.\n    ///\n    /// Uses `kill -0` semantics: sends signal 0 to check existence without\n    /// actually sending a signal.\n    pub fn check_pid_alive(pid: u32) -> bool {\n        std::process::Command::new(\"kill\")\n            .args([\"-0\", &pid.to_string()])\n            .stdout(std::process::Stdio::null())\n            .stderr(std::process::Stdio::null())\n            .status()\n            .map(|s| s.success())\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Detect peers with stale heartbeats.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a list of stale peers, excluding this worker.\n    pub fn detect_stale_peers(&self) -> Result<Vec<StalePeer>> {\n        let heartbeats = Self::read_all_heartbeats(&self.heartbeat_dir)?;\n        let mut stale = Vec::new();\n\n        for hb in heartbeats {\n            // Skip our own heartbeat.\n            if hb.qualified_id == self.qualified_id {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            if Self::is_stale(&hb, self.heartbeat_ttl) {\n                let pid_alive = Self::check_pid_alive(hb.pid);\n                let hb_file = hb.heartbeat_file.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                    self.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", hb.qualified_id))\n                });\n                stale.push(StalePeer {\n                    worker_id: hb.worker_id.clone(),\n                    qualified_id: Some(hb.qualified_id.clone()),\n                    pid: hb.pid,\n                    pid_alive,\n                    current_bead: hb.current_bead.clone(),\n                    last_heartbeat: hb.last_heartbeat,\n                    heartbeat_file: hb_file,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(stale)\n    }\n\n    // ── Internal ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Write a heartbeat file atomically (write temp, then rename).\n    fn write_heartbeat(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let (state, current_bead, beads_processed) = {\n            let guard = self\n                .shared_state\n                .lock()\n                .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"shared state lock poisoned: {e}\"))?;\n            (\n                guard.state.clone(),\n                guard.current_bead.clone(),\n                guard.beads_processed,\n            )\n        };\n\n        let data = HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: self.worker_id.clone(),\n            qualified_id: self.qualified_id.clone(),\n            pid: std::process::id(),\n            state,\n            current_bead,\n            workspace: self.workspace.clone(),\n            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n            started_at: self.started_at,\n            beads_processed,\n            session: self.worker_id.clone(),\n            heartbeat_file: None,\n        };\n\n        let path = self.heartbeat_path();\n        let tmp_path = path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n\n        // Auto-create parent directory so that heartbeats self-recover if the\n        // directory is deleted while a worker is running.\n        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to create heartbeat dir: {}\", parent.display()))?;\n        }\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&data).context(\"failed to serialize heartbeat\")?;\n        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, json.as_bytes()).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\n                \"failed to write temp heartbeat file: {}\",\n                tmp_path.display()\n            )\n        })?;\n        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\n                \"failed to rename heartbeat file: {} -> {}\",\n                tmp_path.display(),\n                path.display()\n            )\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Drop for HealthMonitor {\n    fn drop(&mut self) {\n        // Best-effort: signal the emitter and clean up the heartbeat file.\n        self.stop();\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// StalePeer\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A peer worker detected as having a stale heartbeat.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct StalePeer {\n    pub worker_id: String,\n    /// Fully-qualified identity of the peer.\n    pub qualified_id: Option<String>,\n    pub pid: u32,\n    pub pid_alive: bool,\n    pub current_bead: Option<BeadId>,\n    pub last_heartbeat: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub heartbeat_file: PathBuf,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Emitter loop (runs in a dedicated std::thread)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Maximum sleep between heartbeat attempts when backing off on failure.\nconst MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF: Duration = Duration::from_secs(5 * 60);\n\n/// Background emitter loop. Writes heartbeat at each interval.\n///\n/// Circuit breaker: after `max_consecutive_failures` consecutive write failures\n/// the loop sets the shutdown flag and exits so the worker terminates instead of\n/// spinning indefinitely.\n///\n/// Backoff: each consecutive failure doubles the inter-attempt sleep, capped at\n/// [`MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF`].\n#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\nfn emitter_loop(\n    shared_state: Arc<Mutex<SharedHeartbeatState>>,\n    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n    worker_id: String,\n    qualified_id: String,\n    workspace: PathBuf,\n    started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    interval: Duration,\n    max_consecutive_failures: u32,\n) {\n    // Ensure the heartbeat directory exists before entering the write loop so\n    // that workers self-recover if ~/.needle/state/heartbeats/ is deleted.\n    if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(&heartbeat_dir) {\n        tracing::error!(\n            error = %e,\n            dir = %heartbeat_dir.display(),\n            \"failed to create heartbeat directory\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    let mut consecutive_failures: u32 = 0;\n    let mut current_sleep = interval;\n\n    loop {\n        std::thread::sleep(current_sleep);\n\n        if shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {\n            tracing::debug!(worker = %worker_id, \"heartbeat emitter shutting down\");\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let (state, current_bead, beads_processed) = match shared_state.lock() {\n            Ok(guard) => (\n                guard.state.clone(),\n                guard.current_bead.clone(),\n                guard.beads_processed,\n            ),\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Mutex poisoned — the main thread panicked. Exit.\n                tracing::error!(\n                    worker = %worker_id,\n                    \"shared state mutex poisoned, heartbeat emitter exiting\"\n                );\n                return;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let data = HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: worker_id.clone(),\n            qualified_id: qualified_id.clone(),\n            pid: std::process::id(),\n            state,\n            current_bead,\n            workspace: workspace.clone(),\n            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n            started_at,\n            beads_processed,\n            session: worker_id.clone(),\n            heartbeat_file: None,\n        };\n\n        let path = heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", qualified_id));\n        let tmp_path = path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n\n        let write_result: anyhow::Result<()> = (|| {\n            let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&data)?;\n            std::fs::write(&tmp_path, json.as_bytes())?;\n            std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path)?;\n            Ok(())\n        })();\n\n        match write_result {\n            Ok(()) => {\n                consecutive_failures = 0;\n                current_sleep = interval;\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                consecutive_failures += 1;\n                tracing::error!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    worker = %worker_id,\n                    consecutive_failures,\n                    max = max_consecutive_failures,\n                    \"heartbeat write failed\"\n                );\n                if consecutive_failures >= max_consecutive_failures {\n                    tracing::error!(\n                        worker = %worker_id,\n                        consecutive_failures,\n                        \"heartbeat emitter circuit breaker triggered — worker will shut down\"\n                    );\n                    shutdown.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n                    return;\n                }\n                // Exponential backoff to reduce log spam before the circuit breaker fires.\n                current_sleep = current_sleep.saturating_mul(2).min(MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Tests\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use std::time::Duration;\n\n    fn test_config(heartbeat_dir: &Path) -> Config {\n        let mut config = Config::default();\n        config.workspace.home = heartbeat_dir\n            .parent()\n            .unwrap()\n            .parent()\n            .unwrap()\n            .to_path_buf();\n        config.health.heartbeat_interval_secs = 1;\n        config.health.heartbeat_ttl_secs = 5;\n        config\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn heartbeat_file_written_on_start() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path().join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let config = test_config(&hb_dir);\n        let mut monitor = HealthMonitor::new(\n            config,\n            \"test-worker\".to_string(),\n            Telemetry::new(\"test\".to_string()),\n        );\n\n        monitor.start_emitter().unwrap();\n\n        // The initial heartbeat is written synchronously in start_emitter().\n        let path = monitor.heartbeat_path();\n        assert!(path.exists(), \"heartbeat file should exist after start\");\n\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&path).unwrap();\n        let data: HeartbeatData = serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(data.worker_id, \"test-worker\");\n        assert_eq!(data.pid, std::process::id());\n\n        monitor.stop();\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn heartbeat_updates_with_shared_state() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path().join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let config = test_config(&hb_dir);\n        let mut monitor = HealthMonitor::new(\n            config,\n            \"state-test\".to_string(),\n            Telemetry::new(\"test\".to_string()),\n        );\n\n        monitor.start_emitter().unwrap();\n\n        // Update shared state.\n        monitor.update_state(&WorkerState::Executing, Some(&BeadId::from(\"needle-abc\")));\n        monitor.update_beads_processed(5);\n\n        // Wait for the emitter to write a new heartbeat.\n        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(1500));\n\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(monitor.heartbeat_path()).unwrap();\n        let data: HeartbeatData = serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(data.state, WorkerState::Executing);\n        assert_eq!(data.current_bead, Some(BeadId::from(\"needle-abc\")));\n        assert_eq!(data.beads_processed, 5);\n\n        monitor.stop();\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn heartbeat_file_removed_on_stop() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path().join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let config = test_config(&hb_dir);\n        let mut monitor = HealthMonitor::new(\n            config,\n            \"stop-test\".to_string(),\n            Telemetry::new(\"test\".to_string()),\n        );\n\n        monitor.start_emitter().unwrap();\n        let path = monitor.heartbeat_path();\n        assert!(path.exists());\n\n        monitor.stop();\n        assert!(\n            !path.exists(),\n            \"heartbeat file should be removed after stop\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn read_all_heartbeats_reads_files() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n\n        // Write two heartbeat files.\n        let hb1 = HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: \"worker-a\".to_string(),\n            qualified_id: \"claude-worker-a\".to_string(),\n            pid: 1000,\n            state: WorkerState::Selecting,\n            current_bead: None,\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n            started_at: Utc::now(),\n            beads_processed: 0,\n            session: \"worker-a\".to_string(),\n            heartbeat_file: None,\n        };\n        let hb2 = HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: \"worker-b\".to_string(),\n            qualified_id: \"claude-worker-b\".to_string(),\n            pid: 2000,\n            state: WorkerState::Executing,\n            current_bead: Some(BeadId::from(\"nd-x\")),\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n            started_at: Utc::now(),\n            beads_processed: 3,\n            session: \"worker-b\".to_string(),\n            heartbeat_file: None,\n        };\n\n        std::fs::write(\n            hb_dir.join(\"worker-a.json\"),\n            serde_json::to_string(&hb1).unwrap(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n        std::fs::write(\n            hb_dir.join(\"worker-b.json\"),\n            serde_json::to_string(&hb2).unwrap(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n        // Non-JSON file should be skipped.\n        std::fs::write(hb_dir.join(\"README.txt\"), \"ignore me\").unwrap();\n\n        let heartbeats = HealthMonitor::read_all_heartbeats(hb_dir).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(heartbeats.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn read_all_heartbeats_nonexistent_dir() {\n        let result = HealthMonitor::read_all_heartbeats(Path::new(\"/nonexistent/dir\"));\n        assert!(result.is_ok());\n        assert!(result.unwrap().is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn is_stale_detects_old_heartbeats() {\n        let mut hb = HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: \"test\".to_string(),\n            qualified_id: \"claude-test\".to_string(),\n            pid: 1,\n            state: WorkerState::Selecting,\n            current_bead: None,\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n            started_at: Utc::now(),\n            beads_processed: 0,\n            session: \"test\".to_string(),\n            heartbeat_file: None,\n        };\n\n        // Fresh heartbeat should not be stale.\n        assert!(!HealthMonitor::is_stale(&hb, Duration::from_secs(300)));\n\n        // Old heartbeat should be stale.\n        hb.last_heartbeat = Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::seconds(600);\n        assert!(HealthMonitor::is_stale(&hb, Duration::from_secs(300)));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn check_pid_alive_current_process() {\n        // Our own PID should be alive.\n        assert!(HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(std::process::id()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn check_pid_alive_nonexistent() {\n        // PID 99999999 is almost certainly not running.\n        assert!(!HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(99_999_999));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn atomic_write_never_produces_partial() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path().join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let config = test_config(&hb_dir);\n        let mut monitor = HealthMonitor::new(\n            config,\n            \"atomic-test\".to_string(),\n            Telemetry::new(\"test\".to_string()),\n        );\n\n        monitor.start_emitter().unwrap();\n\n        // Read the heartbeat file multiple times while it's being updated.\n        for _ in 0..10 {\n            let path = monitor.heartbeat_path();\n            if path.exists() {\n                let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&path).unwrap();\n                // Should always be valid JSON (never a partial write).\n                let result: Result<HeartbeatData, _> = serde_json::from_str(&content);\n                assert!(\n                    result.is_ok(),\n                    \"heartbeat file should always be valid JSON, got: {}\",\n                    content\n                );\n            }\n            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));\n        }\n\n        monitor.stop();\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn heartbeat_data_roundtrip() {\n        let data = HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: \"test-rt\".to_string(),\n            qualified_id: \"claude-test-rt\".to_string(),\n            pid: 42,\n            state: WorkerState::Executing,\n            current_bead: Some(BeadId::from(\"nd-abc\")),\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/home/test\"),\n            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n            started_at: Utc::now(),\n            beads_processed: 10,\n Used by both MendStrand (for home) and\n37\t/// ExploreStrand (for remote workspaces).\n38\t///\n39\t/// # Arguments\n40\t/// * `store` - The bead store to scan (can be home or remote)\n41\t/// * `registry` - Worker registry for live worker lookup\n42\t/// * `telemetry` - Telemetry emitter for orphan release events\n43\t/// * `worker_id` - This worker's ID (excluded from orphan detection)\n44\t///\n45\t/// # Returns\n46\t/// * `Ok(u32)` - Number of orphans released\n47\t/// * `Err(anyhow::Error)` - Store read failure\n48\tpub async fn cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n49\t    store: &dyn BeadStore,\n50\t    registry: &Registry,\n51\t    telemetry: &Telemetry,\n52\t    worker_id: &str,\n53\t) -> Result<u32> {\n54\t    let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n55\t    let workers = registry.list()?;\n56\t\n57\t    // Build a set of (worker_id, is_alive) for registered workers.\n58\t    let live_worker_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = workers\n59\t        .iter()\n60\t        .filter(|w| HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(w.pid))\n61\t        .map(|w| w.id.clone())\n62\t        .collect();\n63\t\n64\t    let mut released = 0u32;\n65\t\n66\t    for bead in &all_beads {\n67\t        if bead.status != BeadStatus::InProgress {\n68\t            continue;\n69\t        }\n70\t\n71\t        let assignee = match &bead.assignee {\n72\t            Some(a) if !a.is_empty() => a,\n73\t            _ => continue,\n74\t        };\n75\t\n76\t        // Skip if the assignee matches our own worker (we're running).\n77\t        if assignee == worker_id {\n78\t            continue;\n79\t        }\n80\t\n81\t        // Skip if the assignee matches a registered, alive worker.\n82\t        if live_worker_ids.contains(assignee.as_str()) {\n83\t            continue;\n84\t        }\n85\t\n86\t        // Orphaned: assignee is not a live registered worker. Release it.\n87\t        tracing::info!(\n88\t            bead_id = %bead.id,\n89\t            assignee = %assignee,\n90\t            workspace = %bead.workspace.display(),\n91\t            \"releasing orphaned in-progress bead (assignee has no live worker)\"\n92\t        );\n93\t\n94\t        match store.release(&bead.id).await {\n95\t            Ok(()) => {\n96\t                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n97\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n98\t                    peer_worker: assignee.clone(),\n99\t                });\n100\t                released += 1;\n101\t            }\n102\t            Err(e) => {\n103\t                tracing::warn!(\n104\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n105\t                    error = %e,\n106\t                    \"failed to release orphaned in-progress bead\"\n107\t                );\n108\t            }\n109\t        }\n110\t    }\n111\t\n112\t    Ok(released)\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t/// Summary of
