cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n246\t        &self,\n247\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n248\t        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n249\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n250\t        let released = super::mend::cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n251\t            store,\n252\t            &self.registry,\n253\t            &self.telemetry,\n254\t            &self.worker_id,\n255\t        )\n256\t        .await?;\n257\t        summary.beads_released += released;\n258\t        Ok(())\n259\t    }\n260\t\n261\t    // ── Step 2: Orphaned lock file removal ───────────────────────────────────\n262\t\n263\t    /// Remove claim lock files that are older than the configured TTL and not\n264\t    /// actively held by any process.\n265\t    fn cleanup_orphaned_locks(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n266\t        let lock_ttl = Duration::from_secs(self.config.lock_ttl_secs);\n267\t\n268\t        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.lock_dir) {\n269\t            Ok(e) => e,\n270\t            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n271\t            Err(e) => {\n272\t                tracing::warn!(\n273\t                    dir = %self.lock_dir.display(),\n274\t                    error = %e,\n275\t                    \"failed to read lock directory\"\n276\t                );\n277\t                return Ok(());\n278\t            }\n279\t        };\n280\t\n281\t        for entry in entries {\n282\t            let entry = match entry {\n283\t                Ok(e) => e,\n284\t                Err(_) => continue,\n285\t            };\n286\t\n287\t            let path = entry.path();\n288\t            let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n289\t                Some(n) => n,\n290\t                None => continue,\n291\t            };\n292\t\n293\t            // Only consider needle claim lock files.\n294\t            if !name.starts_with(\"needle-claim-\") || !name.ends_with(\".lock\") {\n295\t                continue;\n296\t            }\n297\t\n298\t            // Check file age.\n299\t            let age = match file_age(&path) {\n300\t                Some(age) => age,\n301\t                None => continue,\n302\t            };\n303\t\n304\t            if age <= lock_ttl {\n305\t                continue;\n306\t            }\n307\t\n308\t            // Try to acquire flock (non-blocking). If we can acquire it,\n309\t            // no one is holding it — safe to delete.\n310\t            match try_acquire_flock(&path) {\n311\t                Ok(Some(_file)) => {\n312\t                    // Lock acquired — no one holds it. Remove the file.\n313\t                    let age_secs = age.as_secs();\n314\t                    if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n315\t                        tracing::warn!(\n316\t                            path = %path.display(),\n317\t                            error = %e,\n318\t                            \"failed to remove orphaned lock file\"\n319\t                        );\n320\t                        continue;\n321\t                    }\n322\t\n323\t                    tracing::info!(\n324\t                        path = %path.display(),\n325\t                        age_secs,\n326\t                        \"removed orphaned lock file\"\n327\t                    );\n328\t\n329\t                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendOrphanedLockRemoved {\n330\t                        lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n331\t                        age_secs,\n332\t                    });\n333\t\n334\t                    summary.locks_removed += 1;\n335\t                }\n336\t                Ok(None) => {\n337\t                    // Lock is actively held — skip.\n338\t                    tracing::debug!(\n339\t                        path = %path.display(),\n340\t                        \"lock file is actively held, skipping\"\n341\t                    );\n342\t                }\n343\t                Err(e) => {\n344\t                    tracing::debug!(\n345\t                        path = %path.display(),\n346\t                        error = %e,\n347\t                        \"failed to probe lock file\"\n348\t                    );\n349\t                }\n350\t            }\n351\t        }\n352\t\n353\t        Ok(())\n354\t    }\n355\t\n356\t    // ── Step 3: Dependency link repair ───────────────────────────────────────\n357\t\n358\t    /// Find open beads that have closed blockers and clean up the stale\n359\t    /// dependency links.\n360\t    ///\n361\t    /// br does not automatically resolve dependency links on bead closure,\n362\t    /// so a bead can remain blocked even after its blocker is closed.\n363\t    async fn cleanup_stale_dependencies(\n364\t        &self,\n365\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n366\t        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n367\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n368\t        let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n369\t\n370\t        for bead in &all_beads {\n371\t            // Only check open beads that have dependencies.\n372\t            if bead.status != BeadStatus::Open || bead.dependencies.is_empty() {\n373\t                continue;\n374\t            }\n375\t\n376\t            for dep in &bead.dependencies {\n377\t                // Only check \"blocks\" type dependencies where the blocker is closed.\n378\t                if dep.dependency_type != \"blocks\" {\n379\t                    continue;\n380\t                }\n381\t\n382\t                if dep.status == \"closed\" {\n383\t                    tracing::info!(\n384\t                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n385\t                        blocker_id = %dep.id,\n386\t                        \"found stale dependency link (closed blocker on open bead)\"\n387\t                    );\n388\t\n389\t                    // Emit telemetry for the cleaned dependency.\n390\t                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendDependencyCleaned {\n391\t                        bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n392\t                        blocker_id: dep.id.clone(),\n393\t                    });\n394\t\n395\t                    summary.deps_cleaned += 1;\n396\t                }\n397\t            }\n398\t        }\n399\t\n400\t        Ok(())\n401\t    }\n402\t\n403\t    // ── Step 4: Agent log file cleanup ──────────────────────────────────────\n404\t\n405\t    /// Delete `.agent.jsonl` files in `log_dir` that are older than\n406\t    /// `retention_days` and do not belong to a currently-executing bead.\n407\t    ///\n408\t    /// Files are matched by the `.agent.jsonl` suffix. The bead ID is parsed\n409\t    /// from the stem (`<worker>-<bead>`): a file is considered active if the\n410\t    /// stem ends with `-<bead_id>` for any in-progress bead.\n411\t    async fn cleanup_old_agent_logs(\n412\t        &self,\n413\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n414\t        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n415\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n416\t        if self.retention_days == 0 {\n417\t            return Ok(());\n418\t        }\n419\t\n420\t        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.log_dir) {\n421\t            Ok(e) => e,\n422\t            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n423\t            Err(e) => {\n424\t                tracing::warn!(\n425\t                    dir = %self.log_dir.display(),\n426\t                    error = %e,\n427\t                    \"mend: failed to read log directory for agent log cleanup\"\n428\t                );\n429\t                return Ok(());\n430\t            }\n431\t        };\n432\t\n433\t        // Collect all .agent.jsonl paths first so we can bail early if none.\n434\t        let agent_log_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = entries\n435\t            .filter_map(|e| e.ok())\n436\t            .map(|e| e.path())\n437\t            .filter(|p| {\n438\t                p.file_name()\n439\t                    .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n440\t                    .map(|n| n.ends_with(\".agent.jsonl\"))\n441\t                    .unwrap_or(false)\n442\t            })\n443\t            .collect();\n444\t\n445\t        if agent_log_paths.is_empty() {\n446\t            return Ok(());\n447\t        }\n448\t\n449\t        let cutoff = Duration::from_secs(u64::from(self.retention_days) * 86400);\n450\t\n451\t        // Build the set of currently in-progress bead IDs.\n452\t        let in_progress_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = store\n453\t            .list_all()\n454\t            .await?\n455\t            .into_iter()\n456\t            .filter(|b| b.status == BeadStatus::InProgress)\n457\t            .map(|b| b.id.as_ref().to_string())\n458\t            .collect();\n459\t\n460\t        for path in &agent_log_paths {\n461\t            // Check file age.\n462\t            let age = match file_age(path) {\n463\t                Some(a) => a,\n464\t                None => continue,\n465\t            };\n466\t\n467\t            if age <= cutoff {\n468\t                continue;\n469\t            }\n470\t\n471\t            // Skip if this log belongs to an in-progress bead.\n472\t            let stem = match path\n473\t                .file_name()\n474\t                .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n475\t                .and_then(|n| n.strip_suffix(\".agent.jsonl\"))\n476\t            {\n477\t                Some(s) => s.to_string(),\n478\t                None => continue,\n479\t            };\n480\t\n481\t            let is_active = in_progress_ids\n482\t                .iter()\n483\t                .any(|bead_id| stem == *bead_id || stem.ends_with(&format!(\"-{bead_id}\")));\n484\t\n485\t            if is_active {\n486\t                tracing::debug!(\n487\t                    path = %path.display(),\n488\t                    \"skipping agent log for in-progress bead\"\n489\t                );\n490\t                continue;\n491\t            }\n492\t\n493\t            if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(path) {\n494\t                tracing::warn!(\n495\t                    path = %path.display(),\n496\t                    error = %e,\n497\t                    \"mend: failed to remove stale agent log\"\n498\t                );\n499\t                continue;\n500\t            }\n501\t\n502\t            tracing::info!(\n503\t                path = %path.display(),\n504\t                age_secs = age.as_secs(),\n505\t                \"mend: removed stale agent log\"\n506\t            );\n507\t            summary.agent_logs_cleaned += 1;\n508\t        }\n509\t\n510\t        Ok(())\n511\t    }\n512\t\n513\t    // ── Step 4.5: Trace retention cleanup ───────────────────────────────────────\n514\t\n515\t    /// Clean up old traces based on retention policy.\n516\t    ///\n517\t    /// - Failed beads (non-zero exit): delete after trace_retention_failed_days\n518\t    /// - Successful beads (exit 0): prune data after trace_retention_success_days, keep metadata only\n519\t    fn cleanup_old_traces(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n520\t        if !self.traces_dir.exists() {\n521\t            return Ok(());\n522\t        }\n523\t\n524\t        match cleanup_traces(\n525\t            &self.traces_dir,\n526\t            self.trace_retention_failed_days,\n527\t            self.trace_retention_success_days,\n528\t        ) {\n529\t            Ok(cleanup_summary) => {\n530\t                summary.traces_pruned = cleanup_summary.traces_pruned;\n531\t                summary.traces_cleaned = cleanup_summary.traces_deleted;\n532\t\n533\t                if cleanup_summary.traces_pruned > 0 || cleanup_summary.traces_deleted > 0 {\n534\t                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendTraceCleanup {\n535\t                        traces_pruned: cleanup_summary.traces_pruned,\n536\t                        traces_deleted: cleanup_summary.traces_deleted,\n537\t                    });\n538\t\n539\t                    tracing::info!(\n540\t                        traces_pruned = cleanup_summary.traces_pruned,\n541\t                        traces_deleted = cleanup_summary.traces_deleted,\n542\t                        \"mend: cleaned up old traces\"\n543\t                    );\n544\t                }\n545\t            }\n546\t            Err(e) => {\n547\t                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: trace cleanup failed\");\n548\t            }\n549\t        }\n550\t\n551\t        Ok(())\n552\t    }\n553\t\n554\t    // ── Step 4.75: Learning consolidation ───────────────────────────────────────\n555\t\n556\t    /// Clean up and consolidate workspace learnings.\n557\t    ///\n558\t    /// 1. Prune stale entries (>90 days without reinforcement)\n559\t    /// 2. Consolidate if entries exceed max_learnings\n560\t    fn cleanup_learnings(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n561\t        let mut learnings = match LearningsFile::load(&self.workspace) {\n562\t            Ok(l) => l,\n563\t            Err(e) => {\n564\t                tracing::debug!(error = %e, \"mend: no learnings file to clean up\");\n565\t                return Ok(());\n566\t            }\n567\t        };\n568\t\n569\t        let _original_count = learnings.entries().len();\n570\t\n571\t        // Step 1: Prune stale entries\n572\t        match learnings.prune_stale() {\n573\t            Ok(pruned) => {\n574\t                if pruned > 0 {\n575\t                    tracing::info!(pruned, \"mend: pruned stale learning entries\");\n576\t                    summary.learnings_pruned = pruned as u32;\n577\t                }\n578\t            }\n579\t            Err(e) => {\n580\t                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: failed to prune stale learnings\");\n581\t            }\n582\t        }\n583\t\n584\t        // Step 2: Consolidate if over limit\n585\t        if learnings.entries().len() > self.max_learnings {\n586\t            match learnings.consolidate(self.max_learnings) {\n587\t                Ok(removed) => {\n588\t                    if removed > 0 {\n589\t                        tracing::info!(\n590\t                            removed,\n591\t                            max_count = self.max_learnings,\n592\t                            \"mend: consolidated learning entries\"\n593\t                        );\n594\t                        summary.learnings_consolidated = removed as u32;\n595\t                    }\n596\t                }\n597\t                Err(e) => {\n598\t                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: failed to consolidate learnings\");\n599\t                }\n600\t            }\n601\t        }\n602\t\n603\t        if summary.learnings_pruned > 0 || summary.learnings_consolidated > 0 {\n604\t            let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendLearningCleanup {\n605\t                pruned: summary.learnings_pruned,\n606\t                consolidated: summary.learnings_consolidated,\n607\t            });\n608\t        }\n609\t\n610\t        Ok(())\n611\t    }\n612\t\n613\t    // ── Step 5: Database health check with auto-recovery ────────────────────\n614\t\n615\t    /// Run `br doctor` to check database health. If issues are found,\n616\t    /// escalate through the recovery pipeline:\n617\t    ///\n618\t    /// 1. `br doctor` (check only) → if warnings found:\n619\t    /// 2. `br doctor --repair` → if fails:\n620\t    /// 3. Full rebuild (rm .beads/beads.db + br sync --import + verify)\n621\t    ///\n622\t    /// If the full rebuild also fails, the JSONL itself may be corrupt.\n623\t    async fn check_db_health(\n624\t        &self,\n625\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n626\t        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n627\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n628\t        // Step 1: Probe with `br doctor` (no repair).\n629\t        let check_result = store.doctor_check().await;\n630\t\n631\t        let needs_repair = match check_result {\n632\t            Ok(report) => {\n633\t                // Doctor succeeded. If there are warnings, escalate to repair.\n634\t                !report.warnings.is_empty()\n635\t            }\n636\t            Err(e) => {\n637\t                let msg = format!(\"{e:#}\");\n638\t                if crate::bead_store::is_corruption_error(&msg) {\n639\t                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"br doctor detected corruption\");\n640\t                    true\n641\t                } else {\n642\t                    // Non-corruption error (e.g., br not found). Propagate.\n643\t                    return Err(e);\n644\t                }\n645\t            }\n646\t        };\n647\t\n648\t        if !needs_repair {\n649\t            return Ok(());\n650\t        }\n651\t\n652\t        // Step 2: Try `br doctor --repair`.\n653\t        tracing::info!(\"database health check found issues, attempting repair\");\n654\t        match store.doctor_repair().await {\n655\t            Ok(report) => {\n656\t                let warnings = report.warnings.len() as u32;\n657\t                let fixed = report.fixed.len() as u32;\n658\t                tracing::info!(warnings, fixed, \"br doctor --repair completed\");\n659\t\n660\t                let _ = self\n661\t                    .telemetry\n662\t                    .emit(EventKind::MendDbRepaired { warnings, fixed });\n663\t\n664\t                summary.db_repaired = true;\n665\t                return Ok(());\n666\t            }\n667\t            Err(e) => {\n668\t                tracing::warn!(\n669\t                    error = %e,\n670\t                    \"br doctor --repair failed, attempting full rebuild\"\n671\t                );\n672\t            }\n673\t        }\n674\t\n675\t        // Step 3: Full rebuild — rm db + br sync --import + verify.\n676\t        match store.full_rebuild().await {\n677\t            Ok(()) => {\n678\t                tracing::info!(\"database fully rebuilt from JSONL\");\n679\t\n680\t                let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendDbRebuilt);\n681\t\n682\t                summary.db_rebuilt = true;\n683\t                Ok(())\n684\t            }\n685\t            Err(e) => {\n686\t                tracing::error!(\n687\t                    error = %e,\n688\t                    \"full database rebuild failed — JSONL may be corrupt\"\n689\t                );\n690\t                Err(e)\n691\t            }\n692\t        }\n693\t    }\n694\t}\n695\t\n696\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n697\timpl super::Strand for MendStrand {\n698\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n699\t        \"mend\"\n700\t    }\n701\t\n702\t    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n703\t        let mut summary = MendSummary::default();\n704\t\n705\t        // Step 1: Stale claim cleanup via peer monitoring.\n706\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_stale_claims(store, &mut summary).await {\n707\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: stale claim cleanup failed\");\n708\t            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n709\t        }\n710\t\n711\t        // Step 1.5: Orphaned in-progress bead recovery (registry-based).\n712\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(store, &mut summary).await {\n713\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: orphaned in-progress cleanup failed\");\n714\t            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n715\t        }\n716\t\n717\t        // Step 2: Orphaned lock file removal.\n718\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_orphaned_locks(&mut summary) {\n719\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: orphaned lock cleanup failed\");\n720\t            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n721\t        }\n722\t\n723\t        // Step 3: Dependency link repair.\n724\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_stale_dependencies(store, &mut summary).await {\n725\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: dependency cleanup failed\");\n726\t            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n727\t        }\n728\t\n729\t        // Step 4: Agent log file cleanup.\n730\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_old_agent_logs(store, &mut summary).await {\n731\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: agent log cleanup failed\");\n732\t            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n733\t        }\n734\t\n735\t        // Step 4.5: Trace retention cleanup.\n736\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_old_traces(&mut summary) {\n737\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: trace cleanup failed\");\n738\t            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n739\t        }\n740\t\n741\t        // Step 4.75: Learning consolidation.\n742\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_learnings(&mut summary) {\n743\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: learning cleanup failed\");\n744\t            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n745\t        }\n746\t\n747\t        // Step 5: Database health check.\n748\t        if let Err(e) = self.check_db_health(store, &mut summary).await {\n749\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: database health check failed\");\n750\t            // DB check failure is non-fatal — continue with the summary.\n751\t        }\n752\t\n753\t        // Emit cycle summary telemetry.\n754\t        let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendCycleSummary {\n755\t            beads_released: summary.beads_released,\n756\t            locks_removed: summary.locks_removed,\n757\t            deps_cleaned: summary.deps_cleaned,\n758\t            db_repaired: summary.db_repaired,\n759\t            db_rebuilt: summary.db_rebuilt,\n760\t            agent_logs_cleaned: summary.agent_logs_cleaned,\n761\t            traces_pruned: summary.traces_pruned,\n762\t            traces_deleted: summary.traces_cleaned,\n763\t        });\n764\t\n765\t        if summary.did_work() {\n766\t            tracing::info!(\n767\t                beads_released = summary.beads_released,\n768\t                locks_removed = summary.locks_removed,\n769\t                deps_cleaned = summary.deps_cleaned,\n770\t                db_repaired = summary.db_repaired,\n771\t                db_rebuilt = summary.db_rebuilt,\n772\t                agent_logs_cleaned = summary.agent_logs_cleaned,\n773\t                traces_pruned = summary.traces_pruned,\n774\t                traces_deleted = summary.traces_cleaned,\n775\t                \"mend performed cleanup — restarting waterfall\"\n776\t            );\n777\t            StrandResult::WorkCreated\n778\t        } else {\n779\t            tracing::debug!(\"mend found nothing to clean\");\n780\t            StrandResult::NoWork\n781\t        }\n782\t    }\n783\t}\n784\t\n785\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n786\t// Helpers\n787\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n788\t\n789\t/// Get the age of a file based on its modification time.\n790\tfn file_age(path: &Path) -> Option<Duration> {\n791\t    let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).ok()?;\n792\t    let modified = metadata.modified().ok()?;\n793\t    SystemTime::now().duration_since(modified).ok()\n794\t}\n795\t\n796\t/// Try to acquire an exclusive flock on a file (non-blocking).\n797\t///\n798\t/// Returns `Ok(Some(file))` if acquired, `Ok(None)` if held by another process.\n799\tfn try_acquire_flock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<std::fs::File>> {\n800\t    use fs2::FileExt;\n801\t\n802\t    let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n803\t        .read(true)\n804\t        .write(true)\n805\t        .open(path)?;\n806\t\n807\t    match file.try_lock_exclusive() {\n808\t        Ok(()) => Ok(Some(file)),\n809\t        Err(ref e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::WouldBlock => Ok(None),\n810\t        Err(e) => Err(e.into()),\n811\t    }\n812\t}\n813\t\n814\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n815\t// Tests\n816\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n817\t\n818\t#[cfg(test)]\n819\tmod tests {\n820\t    use super::*;\n821\t    use crate::bead_store::{Filters, RepairReport};\n822\t    use crate::health::HeartbeatData;\n823\t    use crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, BrDependency, ClaimResult, WorkerState};\n824\t\n825\t    use async_trait::async_trait;\n826\t    use chrono::{TimeZone, Utc};\n827\t    use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};\n828\t    use std::sync::Arc;\n829\t\n830\t    // ── Mock bead store ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n831\t\n832\t    struct MockBeadStore {\n833\t        all_beads: Vec<Bead>,\n834\t        release_count: Arc<AtomicU32>,\n835\t        /// Warnings returned by doctor_check (probe-only).\n836\t        check_warnings: Vec<String>,\n837\t        /// If Some, doctor_repair succeeds with this report. If None, it fails.\n838\t        repair_report: Option<RepairReport>,\n839\t        /// Whether full_rebuild() should fail.\n840\t        rebuild_fails: bool,\n841\t    }\n842\t\n843\t    impl MockBeadStore {\n844\t        fn new(beads: Vec<Bead>) -> (Self, Arc<AtomicU32>) {\n845\t            let release_count = Arc::new(AtomicU32::new(0));\n846\t            (\n847\t                MockBeadStore {\n848\t                    all_beads: beads,\n849\t                    release_count: release_count.clone(),\n850\t                    check_warnings: vec![],\n851\t                    repair_report: Some(RepairReport::default()),\n852\t                    rebuild_fails: false,\n853\t                },\n854\t                release_count,\n855\t            )\n856\t           \"non-agent log files must not be touched\"\n1975\t        );\n1976\t    }\n1977\t}\n1978\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8d2ee6f9-9054-4e1b-b4ba-04f7ef472c61","uuid":"b616b928-746a-4f27-8fa5-69d61c32899f","timestamp":"2026-04-24T12:32:46.826Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/mend.rs","content":"//! Mend strand: maintenance and self-healing.\n//!\n//! Strand 2 in the waterfall. Runs after Pluck finds no work. Cleans up\n//! stale claims, orphaned locks, broken dependency links, and database\n//! corruption. If any cleanup is performed, returns `WorkCreated` so the\n//! waterfall restarts from Pluck (released beads may now be claimable).\n//!\n//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `config`, `health`, `peer`, `registry`,\n//!             `telemetry`, `types`, `trace`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::time::{Duration, SystemTime};\n\nuse anyhow::Result;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::config::MendConfig;\nuse crate::health::HealthMonitor;\nuse crate::learning::LearningsFile;\nuse crate::peer::PeerMonitor;\nuse crate::registry::Registry;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::trace::cleanup_traces;\nuse crate::types::{BeadStatus, StrandError, StrandResult};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Store-scoped orphan cleanup (shared between Mend and Explore)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Scan all in-progress beads in a store and release any whose assignee does\n/// not correspond to a live worker. This catches beads orphaned by workers\n/// that died without leaving a heartbeat file (or whose heartbeat was already\n/// cleaned up).\n///\n/// This is a store-scoped function that can be called against any BeadStore,\n/// not just the home workspace. Used by both MendStrand (for home) and\n/// ExploreStrand (for remote workspaces).\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `store` - The bead store to scan (can be home or remote)\n/// * `registry` - Worker registry for live worker lookup\n/// * `telemetry` - Telemetry emitter for orphan release events\n/// * `worker_id` - This worker's ID (excluded from orphan detection)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(u32)` - Number of orphans released\n/// * `Err(anyhow::Error)` - Store read failure\npub async fn cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n    store: &dyn BeadStore,\n    registry: &Registry,\n    telemetry: &Telemetry,\n    worker_id: &str,\n) -> Result<u32> {\n    let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n    let workers = registry.list()?;\n\n    // Build a set of (worker_id, is_alive) for registered workers.\n    let live_worker_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = workers\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|w| HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(w.pid))\n        .map(|w| w.id.clone())\n        .collect();\n\n    let mut released = 0u32;\n\n    for bead in &all_beads {\n        if bead.status != BeadStatus::InProgress {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let assignee = match &bead.assignee {\n            Some(a) if !a.is_empty() => a,\n            _ => continue,\n        };\n\n        // Skip if the assignee matches our own worker (we're running).\n        if assignee == worker_id {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Skip if the assignee matches a registered, alive worker.\n        if live_worker_ids.contains(assignee.as_str()) {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Orphaned: assignee is not a live registered worker. Release it.\n        tracing::info!(\n            bead_id = %bead.id,\n            assignee = %assignee,\n            workspace = %bead.workspace.display(),\n            \"releasing orphaned in-progress bead (assignee has no live worker)\"\n        );\n\n        match store.release(&bead.id).await {\n            Ok(()) => {\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    peer_worker: assignee.clone(),\n                });\n                released += 1;\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to release orphaned in-progress bead\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(released)\n}\n\n/// Summary of work performed during one Mend evaluation cycle.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct MendSummary {\n    beads_released: u32,\n    locks_removed: u32,\n    deps_cleaned: u32,\n    db_repaired: bool,\n    db_rebuilt: bool,\n    agent_logs_cleaned: u32,\n    traces_pruned: u32,\n    traces_cleaned: u32,\n    learnings_pruned: u32,\n    learnings_consolidated: u32,\n}\n\nimpl MendSummary {\n    /// Whether any cleanup work was performed.\n    fn did_work(&self) -> bool {\n        self.beads_released > 0\n            || self.locks_removed > 0\n            || self.deps_cleaned > 0\n            || self.db_repaired\n            || self.db_rebuilt\n            || self.agent_logs_cleaned > 0\n            || self.traces_pruned > 0\n            || self.traces_cleaned > 0\n            || self.learnings_pruned > 0\n            || self.learnings_consolidated > 0\n    }\n}\n\n/// The Mend strand — maintenance and self-healing.\npub struct MendStrand {\n    config: MendConfig,\n    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n    lock_dir: PathBuf,\n    worker_id: String,\n    registry: Registry,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n    log_dir: PathBuf,\n    retention_days: u32,\n    traces_dir: PathBuf,\n    trace_retention_failed_days: u32,\n    trace_retention_success_days: u32,\n    workspace: PathBuf,\n    max_learnings: usize,\n}\n\nimpl MendStrand {\n    /// Create a new MendStrand.\n    ///\n    /// - `config`: mend strand configuration\n    /// - `heartbeat_dir`: path to `~/.needle/state/heartbeats/`\n    /// - `heartbeat_ttl`: how long before a heartbeat is considered stale\n    /// - `lock_dir`: directory where claim lock files live (default: `/tmp`)\n    /// - `worker_id`: this worker's ID (excluded from peer checks)\n    /// - `registry`: worker state registry\n    /// - `telemetry`: telemetry emitter\n    /// - `log_dir`: directory where agent log files live\n    /// - `retention_days`: number of days to retain agent log files (0 = disabled)\n    /// - `traces_dir`: directory where trace files live (`.beads/traces`)\n    /// - `trace_retention_failed_days`: retention days for failed bead traces\n    /// - `trace_retention_success_days`: retention days for successful bead traces\n    /// - `workspace`: workspace root path for learning consolidation\n    /// - `max_learnings`: maximum number of learning entries before consolidation\n    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n    pub fn new(\n        config: MendConfig,\n        heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n        heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n        lock_dir: PathBuf,\n        worker_id: String,\n        registry: Registry,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n        log_dir: PathBuf,\n        retention_days: u32,\n        traces_dir: PathBuf,\n        trace_retention_failed_days: u32,\n        trace_retention_success_days: u32,\n        workspace: PathBuf,\n        max_learnings: usize,\n    ) -> Self {\n        MendStrand {\n            config,\n            heartbeat_dir,\n            heartbeat_ttl,\n            lock_dir,\n            worker_id,\n            registry,\n            telemetry,\n            log_dir,\n            retention_days,\n            traces_dir,\n            trace_retention_failed_days,\n            trace_retention_success_days,\n            workspace,\n            max_learnings,\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 1: Stale claim cleanup via peer monitoring ──────────────────────\n\n    /// Check for crashed workers and release their orphaned beads.\n    async fn cleanup_stale_claims(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let peer_monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            self.heartbeat_dir.clone(),\n            self.heartbeat_ttl,\n            self.worker_id.clone(),\n            store,\n            &self.registry,\n            self.telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let peer_result = peer_monitor.check_peers().await?;\n        summary.beads_released += peer_result.beads_released;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 1.5: Orphaned in-progress bead recovery ────────────────────────\n\n    /// Scan all in-progress beads and release any whose assignee does not\n    /// correspond to a live worker. This catches beads orphaned by workers\n    /// that died without leaving a heartbeat file (or whose heartbeat was\n    /// already cleaned up).\n    async fn cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let released = super::mend::cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n            store,\n            &self.registry,\n            &self.telemetry,\n            &self.worker_id,\n        )\n        .await?;\n        summary.beads_released += released;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 2: Orphaned lock file removal ───────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Remove claim lock files that are older than the configured TTL and not\n    /// actively held by any process.\n    fn cleanup_orphaned_locks(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        let lock_ttl = Duration::from_secs(self.config.lock_ttl_secs);\n\n        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.lock_dir) {\n            Ok(e) => e,\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    dir = %self.lock_dir.display(),\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to read lock directory\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        for entry in entries {\n            let entry = match entry {\n                Ok(e) => e,\n                Err(_) => continue,\n            };\n\n            let path = entry.path();\n            let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n                Some(n) => n,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            // Only consider needle claim lock files.\n            if !name.starts_with(\"needle-claim-\") || !name.ends_with(\".lock\") {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check file age.\n            let age = match file_age(&path) {\n                Some(age) => age,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            if age <= lock_ttl {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Try to acquire flock (non-blocking). If we can acquire it,\n            // no one is holding it — safe to delete.\n            match try_acquire_flock(&path) {\n                Ok(Some(_file)) => {\n                    // Lock acquired — no one holds it. Remove the file.\n                    let age_secs = age.as_secs();\n                    if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            path = %path.display(),\n                            error = %e,\n                            \"failed to remove orphaned lock file\"\n                        );\n                        continue;\n                    }\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        path = %path.display(),\n                        age_secs,\n                        \"removed orphaned lock file\"\n                    );\n\n                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendOrphanedLockRemoved {\n                        lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n                        age_secs,\n                    });\n\n                    summary.locks_removed += 1;\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    // Lock is actively held — skip.\n                    tracing::debug!(\n                        path = %path.display(),\n                        \"lock file is actively held, skipping\"\n                    );\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::debug!(\n                        path = %path.display(),\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"failed to probe lock file\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 3: Dependency link repair ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Find open beads that have closed blockers and clean up the stale\n    /// dependency links.\n    ///\n    /// br does not automatically resolve dependency links on bead closure,\n    /// so a bead can remain blocked even after its blocker is closed.\n    async fn cleanup_stale_dependencies(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n\n        for bead in &all_beads {\n            // Only check open beads that have dependencies.\n            if bead.status != BeadStatus::Open || bead.dependencies.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            for dep in &bead.dependencies {\n                // Only check \"blocks\" type dependencies where the blocker is closed.\n                if dep.dependency_type != \"blocks\" {\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                if dep.status == \"closed\" {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n                        blocker_id = %dep.id,\n                        \"found stale dependency link (closed blocker on open bead)\"\n                    );\n\n                    // Emit telemetry for the cleaned dependency.\n                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendDependencyCleaned {\n                        bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                        blocker_id: dep.id.clone(),\n                    });\n\n                    summary.deps_cleaned += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 4: Agent log file cleanup ──────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Delete `.agent.jsonl` files in `log_dir` that are older than\n    /// `retention_days` and do not belong to a currently-executing bead.\n    ///\n    /// Files are matched by the `.agent.jsonl` suffix. The bead ID is parsed\n    /// from the stem (`<worker>-<bead>`): a file is considered active if the\n    /// stem ends with `-<bead_id>` for any in-progress bead.\n    async fn cleanup_old_agent_logs(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        if self.retention_days == 0 {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.log_dir) {\n            Ok(e) => e,\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    dir = %self.log_dir.display(),\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"mend: failed to read log directory for agent log cleanup\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Collect all .agent.jsonl paths first so we can bail early if none.\n        let agent_log_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = entries\n            .filter_map(|e| e.ok())\n            .map(|e| e.path())\n            .filter(|p| {\n                p.file_name()\n                    .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n                    .map(|n| n.ends_with(\".agent.jsonl\"))\n                    .unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        if agent_log_paths.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        let cutoff = Duration::from_secs(u64::from(self.retention_days) * 86400);\n\n        // Build the set of currently in-progress bead IDs.\n        let in_progress_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = store\n            .list_all()\n            .await?\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter(|b| b.status == BeadStatus::InProgress)\n            .map(|b| b.id.as_ref().to_string())\n            .collect();\n\n        for path in &agent_log_paths {\n            // Check file age.\n            let age = match file_age(path) {\n                Some(a) => a,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            if age <= cutoff {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip if this log belongs to an in-progress bead.\n            let stem = match path\n                .file_name()\n                .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n                .and_then(|n| n.strip_suffix(\".agent.jsonl\"))\n            {\n                Some(s) => s.to_string(),\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            let is_active = in_progress_ids\n                .iter()\n                .any(|bead_id| stem == *bead_id || stem.ends_with(&format!(\"-{bead_id}\")));\n\n            if is_active {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    path = %path.display(),\n                    \"skipping agent log for in-progress bead\"\n                );\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(path) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    path = %path.display(),\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"mend: failed to remove stale agent log\"\n                );\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            tracing::info!(\n                path = %path.display(),\n                age_secs = age.as_secs(),\n                \"mend: removed stale agent log\"\n            );\n            summary.agent_logs_cleaned += 1;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 4.5: Trace retention cleanup ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Clean up old traces based on retention policy.\n    ///\n    /// - Failed beads (non-zero exit): delete after trace_retention_failed_days\n    /// - Successful beads (exit 0): prune data after trace_retention_success_days, keep metadata only\n    fn cleanup_old_traces(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        if !self.traces_dir.exists() {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        match cleanup_traces(\n            &self.traces_dir,\n            self.trace_retention_failed_days,\n            self.trace_retention_success_days,\n        ) {\n            Ok(cleanup_summary) => {\n                summary.traces_pruned = cleanup_summary.traces_pruned;\n                summary.traces_cleaned = cleanup_summary.traces_deleted;\n\n                if cleanup_summary.traces_pruned > 0 || cleanup_summary.traces_deleted > 0 {\n                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendTraceCleanup {\n                        traces_pruned: cleanup_summary.traces_pruned,\n                        traces_deleted: cleanup_summary.traces_deleted,\n                    });\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        traces_pruned = cleanup_summary.traces_pruned,\n                        traces_deleted = cleanup_summary.traces_deleted,\n                        \"mend: cleaned up old traces\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: trace cleanup failed\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 4.75: Learning consolidation ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Clean up and consolidate workspace learnings.\n    ///\n    /// 1. Prune stale entries (>90 days without reinforcement)\n    /// 2. Consolidate if entries exceed max_learnings\n    fn cleanup_learnings(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut learnings = match LearningsFile::load(&self.workspace) {\n            Ok(l) => l,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::debug!(error = %e, \"mend: no learnings file to clean up\");\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        let _original_count = learnings.entries().len();\n\n        // Step 1: Prune stale entries\n        match learnings.prune_stale() {\n            Ok(pruned) => {\n                if pruned > 0 {\n                    tracing::info!(pruned, \"mend: pruned stale learning entries\");\n                    summary.learnings_pruned = pruned as u32;\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: failed to prune stale learnings\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: Consolidate if over limit\n        if learnings.entries().len() > self.max_learnings {\n            match learnings.consolidate(self.max_learnings) {\n                Ok(removed) => {\n                    if removed > 0 {\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            removed,\n                            max_count = self.max_learnings,\n                            \"mend: consolidated learning entries\"\n                        );\n                        summary.learnings_consolidated = removed as u32;\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: failed to consolidate learnings\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if summary.learnings_pruned > 0 || summary.learnings_consolidated > 0 {\n            let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendLearningCleanup {\n                pruned: summary.learnings_pruned,\n                consolidated: summary.learnings_consolidated,\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 5: Database health check with auto-recovery ────────────────────\n\n    /// Run `br doctor` to check database health. If issues are found,\n    /// escalate through the recovery pipeline:\n    ///\n    /// 1. `br doctor` (check only) → if warnings found:\n    /// 2. `br doctor --repair` → if fails:\n    /// 3. Full rebuild (rm .beads/beads.db + br sync --import + verify)\n    ///\n    /// If the full rebuild also fails, the JSONL itself may be corrupt.\n    async fn check_db_health(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        // Step 1: Probe with `br doctor` (no repair).\n        let check_result = store.doctor_check().await;\n\n        let needs_repair = match check_result {\n            Ok(report) => {\n                // Doctor succeeded. If there are warnings, escalate to repair.\n                !report.warnings.is_empty()\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                let msg = format!(\"{e:#}\");\n                if crate::bead_store::is_corruption_error(&msg) {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"br doctor detected corruption\");\n                    true\n                } else {\n                    // Non-corruption error (e.g., br not found). Propagate.\n                    return Err(e);\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        if !needs_repair {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: Try `br doctor --repair`.\n        tracing::info!(\"database health check found issues, attempting repair\");\n        match store.doctor_repair().await {\n            Ok(report) => {\n                let warnings = report.warnings.len() as u32;\n                let fixed = report.fixed.len() as u32;\n                tracing::info!(warnings, fixed, \"br doctor --repair completed\");\n\n                let _ = self\n                    .telemetry\n                    .emit(EventKind::MendDbRepaired { warnings, fixed });\n\n                summary.db_repaired = true;\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"br doctor --repair failed, attempting full rebuild\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 3: Full rebuild — rm db + br sync --import + verify.\n        match store.full_rebuild().await {\n            Ok(()) => {\n                tracing::info!(\"database fully rebuilt from JSONL\");\n\n                let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendDbRebuilt);\n\n                summary.db_rebuilt = true;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"full database rebuild failed — JSONL may be corrupt\"\n                );\n                Err(e)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl super::Strand for MendStrand {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"mend\"\n    }\n\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n        let mut summary = MendSummary::default();\n\n        // Step 1: Stale claim cleanup via peer monitoring.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_stale_claims(store, &mut summary).await {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: stale claim cleanup failed\");\n            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n        }\n\n        // Step 1.5: Orphaned in-progress bead recovery (registry-based).\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(store, &mut summary).await {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: orphaned in-progress cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: Orphaned lock file removal.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_orphaned_locks(&mut summary) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: orphaned lock cleanup failed\");\n            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n        }\n\n        // Step 3: Dependency link repair.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_stale_dependencies(store, &mut summary).await {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: dependency cleanup failed\");\n            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Agent log file cleanup.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_old_agent_logs(store, &mut summary).await {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: agent log cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 4.5: Trace retention cleanup.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_old_traces(&mut summary) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: trace cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 4.75: Learning consolidation.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_learnings(&mut summary) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: learning cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 5: Database health check.\n        if let Err(e) = self.check_db_health(store, &mut summary).await {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: database health check failed\");\n            // DB check failure is non-fatal — continue with the summary.\n        }\n\n        // Emit cycle summary telemetry.\n        let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendCycleSummary {\n            beads_released: summary.beads_released,\n            locks_removed: summary.locks_removed,\n            deps_cleaned: summary.deps_cleaned,\n            db_repaired: summary.db_repaired,\n            db_rebuilt: summary.db_rebuilt,\n            agent_logs_cleaned: summary.agent_logs_cleaned,\n            traces_pruned: summary.traces_pruned,\n            traces_deleted: summary.traces_cleaned,\n        });\n\n        if summary.did_work() {\n            tracing::info!(\n                beads_released = summary.beads_released,\n                locks_removed = summary.locks_removed,\n                deps_cleaned = summary.deps_cleaned,\n                db_repaired = summary.db_repaired,\n                db_rebuilt = summary.db_rebuilt,\n                agent_logs_cleaned = summary.agent_logs_cleaned,\n                traces_pruned = summary.traces_pruned,\n                traces_deleted = summary.traces_cleaned,\n                \"mend performed cleanup — restarting waterfall\"\n            );\n            StrandResult::WorkCreated\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\"mend found nothing to clean\");\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Helpers\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Get the age of a file based on its modification time.\nfn file_age(path: &Path) -> Option<Duration> {\n    let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).ok()?;\n    let modified = metadata.modified().ok()?;\n    SystemTime::now().duration_since(modified).ok()\n}\n\n/// Try to acquire an exclusive flock on a file (non-blocking).\n///\n/// Returns `Ok(Some(file))` if acquired, `Ok(None)` if held by another process.\nfn try_acquire_flock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<std::fs::File>> {\n    use fs2::FileExt;\n\n    let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n        .read(true)\n        .write(true)\n        .open(path)?;\n\n    match file.try_lock_exclusive() {\n        Ok(()) => Ok(Some(file)),\n        Err(ref e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::WouldBlock => Ok(None),\n        Err(e) => Err(e.into()),\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Tests\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::{Filters, RepairReport};\n    use crate::health::HeartbeatData;\n    use crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, BrDependency, ClaimResult, WorkerState};\n\n    use async_trait::async_trait;\n    use chrono::{TimeZone, Utc};\n    use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};\n    use std::sync::Arc;\n\n    // ── Mock bead store ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    struct MockBeadStore {\n        all_beads: Vec<Bead>,\n        release_count: Arc<AtomicU32>,\n        /// Warnings returned by doctor_check (probe-only).\n        check_warnings: Vec<String>,\n        /// If Some, doctor_repair succeeds with this report. If None, it fails.\n        repair_report: Option<RepairReport>,\n        /// Whether full_rebuild() should fail.\n        rebuild_fails: bool,\n    }\n\n    impl MockBeadStore {\n        fn new(beads: Vec<Bead>) -> (Self, Arc<AtomicU32>) {\n            let release_count = Arc::new(AtomicU32::new(0));\n            (\n                MockBeadStore {\n                    all_beads: beads,\n                    release_count: release_count.clone(),\n                    check_warnings: vec![],\n                    repair_report: Some(RepairReport::default()),\n                    rebuild_fails: false,\n                },\n                release_count,\n            )\n        }\n\n        /// doctor_check returns warnings → escalates to repair (which succeeds).\n        fn with_doctor_report(mut self, report: RepairReport) -> Self {\n            self.check_warnings = report.warnings.clone();\n            self.repair_report = Some(report);\n            self\n        }\n\n        /// doctor_check returns warnings, doctor_repair fails → full rebuild.\n        fn with_repair_failure(mut self) -> Self {\n            self.check_warnings = vec![\"corruption detected\".to_string()];\n            self.repair_report = None;\n            self.rebuild_fails = false;\n            self\n        }\n\n        /// Everything fails → persistent corruption (ERRORED state).\n        fn with_all_recovery_failure(mut self) -> Self {\n            self.check_warnings = vec![\"corruption detected\".to_string()];\n            self.repair_report = None;\n            self.rebuild_fails = true;\n            self\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for MockBeadStore {\n        async fn ready(&self, _filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(self.all_beads.clone())\n        }\n        async fn show(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented in mock\")\n        }\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented in mock\")\n        }\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            self.release_count.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn labels(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn remove_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"mock-bead\"))\n        }\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            match &self.repair_report {\n                Some(r) => Ok(RepairReport {\n                    warnings: r.warnings.clone(),\n                    fixed: r.fixed.clone(),\n                }),\n                None => anyhow::bail!(\"database disk image is malformed\"),\n            }\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport {\n                warnings: self.check_warnings.clone(),\n                fixed: vec![],\n            })\n        }\n        async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            if self.rebuild_fails {\n                anyhow::bail!(\"full rebuild failed: JSONL corrupt\")\n            } else {\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n        async fn add_dependency(&self, _blocker_id: &BeadId, _blocked_id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Failing bead store for error-path tests.\n    struct FailingStore;\n\n    #[async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for FailingStore {\n        async fn ready(&self, _filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn show(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn labels(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn remove_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store error\")\n        }\n        async fn add_dependency(&self, _blocker_id: &BeadId, _blocked_id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    fn make_mend_strand(hb_dir: &Path, lock_dir: &Path, reg_dir: &Path) -> MendStrand {\n        MendStrand::new(\n            MendConfig::default(),\n            hb_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            lock_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            \"test-worker\".to_string(),\n            Registry::new(reg_dir),\n            Telemetry::new(\"test-worker\".to_string()),\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/needle-test-logs\"),\n            0,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-traces\"),\n            30,\n            7,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-workspace\"),\n            80,\n        )\n    }\n\n    fn make_bead_with_deps(id: &str, status: BeadStatus, deps: Vec<BrDependency>) -> Bead {\n        let dt = Utc.with_ymd_and_hms(2026, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0).unwrap();\n        Bead {\n            id: BeadId::from(id),\n            title: format!(\"Bead {id}\"),\n            body: None,\n            priority: 1,\n            status,\n            assignee: None,\n            labels: vec![],\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            dependencies: deps,\n            dependents: vec![],\n            created_at: dt,\n            updated_at: dt,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_dep(id: &str, status: &str, dep_type: &str) -> BrDependency {\n        BrDependency {\n            id: BeadId::from(id),\n            title: format!(\"Dep {id}\"),\n            status: status.to_string(),\n            priority: 1,\n            dependency_type: dep_type.to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_in_progress_bead(id: &str, assignee: &str) -> Bead {\n        let dt = Utc.with_ymd_and_hms(2026, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0).unwrap();\n        Bead {\n            id: BeadId::from(id),\n            title: format!(\"Bead {id}\"),\n            body: None,\n            priority: 1,\n            status: BeadStatus::InProgress,\n            assignee: Some(assignee.to_string()),\n            labels: vec![],\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            dependencies: vec![],\n            dependents: vec![],\n            created_at: dt,\n            updated_at: dt,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn write_heartbeat(dir: &Path, data: &HeartbeatData) {\n        let name = if data.qualified_id.is_empty() {\n            &data.worker_id\n        } else {\n            &data.qualified_id\n        };\n        let path = dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", name));\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(data).unwrap();\n        std::fs::write(path, json).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    fn make_stale_heartbeat(worker_id: &str, pid: u32, bead_id: Option<&str>) -> HeartbeatData {\n        HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n            qualified_id: format!(\"claude-{}\", worker_id),\n            pid,\n            state: WorkerState::Executing,\n            current_bead: bead_id.map(BeadId::from),\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            last_heartbeat: Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::seconds(600),\n            started_at: Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::seconds(3600),\n            beads_processed: 0,\n            session: worker_id.to_string(),\n            heartbeat_file: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    use super::super::Strand;\n\n    // ── Stale claim cleanup tests ────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn crashed_peer_bead_released_returns_work_created() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Write a stale heartbeat with dead PID.\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir.path(),\n            &make_stale_heartbeat(\"dead-worker\", 99_999_999, Some(\"nd-orphan\")),\n        );\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new(vec![]);\n        let mend = make_mend_strand(hb_dir.path(), lock_dir.path(), reg_dir.path());\n\n        let result = mend.evaluate(&store).await;\n        assert!(\n            matches!(result, StrandResult::WorkCreated),\n            \"expected WorkCreated after releasing crashed peer's bead, got: {result:?}\"\n        );\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn no_stale_peers_returns_no_work() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        let (store, _) = MockBeadStore::new(vec![]);\n        let mend = make_mend_strand(hb_dir.path(), lock_dir.path(), reg_dir.path());\n\n        let result = mend.evaluate(&store).await;\n        assert!(\n            matches!(result, StrandResult::NoWork),\n            \"expected NoWork when nothing to clean, got: {result:?}\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // ── Orphaned in-progress bead tests ─────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn orphaned_in_progress_bead_released() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn in_progress_bead_with_live_worker_not_released() {\n        let hb_dir =    let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // An in-progress bead assigned to ourselves.\n        let bead = releasing bead from dead registered worker, got: {result:?}\"\n        );\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 1);\n    }\n\n    // ── Qualified ID collision tests ────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// When two workers from different adapter pools share a NATO name (e.g.\n    /// \"foxtrot\"), their qualified IDs differ (e.g. \"glm-5-foxtrot\" vs\n    /// \"glm-4_7-foxtrot\"). A bead assigned to one must NOT be treated as\n    /// orphaned just because the other worker of the same NATO name is alive.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn collision_same_nato_different_adapter_live_worker_not_released() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Two workers share NATO name \"foxtrot\" but have different adapters.\n        let glm5_foxtrot = \"claude-code-glm-5-foxtrot\";\n        let glm47_foxtrot = \"claude-code-glm-4_7-foxtrot\";\n\n        // A bead assigned to glm-5-foxtrot.\n        let bead = make_in_progress_bead(\"nd-collision-a\", glm5_foxtrot);\n\n        // Register BOTH workers as alive.\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        registry\n            .register(crate::registry::WorkerEntry {\n                id: glm5_foxtrot.to_string(),\n                pid: std::process::id(),\n                workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n                agent: \"claude-code-glm-5\".to_string(),\n                model: None,\n                provider: None,\n                started_at: Utc::now(),\n                beads_processed: 0,\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n        registry\n            .register(crate::registry::WorkerEntry {\n                id: glm47_foxtrot.to_string(),\n                pid: std::process::id(),\n                workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n                agent: \"claude-code-glm-4_7\".to_string(),\n                model: None,\n                provider: None,\n                started_at: Utc::now(),\n                beads_processed: 100,\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Run mend as glm-4_7-foxtrot.\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new(vec![bead]);\n        let mend = MendStrand::new(\n            MendConfig::default(),\n            hb_dir.path().to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            lock_dir.path().to_path_buf(),\n            glm47_foxtrot.to_string(),\n            registry,\n            Telemetry::new(glm47_foxtrot.to_string()),\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/needle-test-logs\"),\n            0,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-traces\"),\n            30,\n            7,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-workspace\"),\n            80,\n        );\n\n        let result = mend.evaluate(&store).await;\n        assert!(\n            matches!(result, StrandResult::NoWork),\n            \"expected NoWork — bead assigned to glm-5-foxtrot should NOT be orphaned, got: {result:?}\"\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed),\n            0,\n            \"bead must not be released when its owner (glm-5-foxtrot) is alive\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// When only one worker of a shared NATO name is dead, the bead assigned\n    /// to the dead worker's qualified ID should be released even though the\n    /// other worker with the same NATO name is alive.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn collision_same_nato_different_adapter_dead_worker_released() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        let glm5_foxtrot = \"claude-code-glm-5-foxtrot\";\n        let glm47_foxtrot = \"claude-code-glm-4_7-foxtrot\";\n\n        // A bead assigned to glm-5-foxtrot (which is dead).\n        let bead = make_in_progress_bead(\"nd-collision-b\", glm5_foxtrot);\n\n        // Register glm-5-foxtrot as DEAD and glm-4_7-foxtrot as alive.\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        registry\n            .register(crate::registry::WorkerEntry {\n                id: glm5_foxtrot.to_string(),\n                pid: 99_999_999, // dead PID\n                workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n                agent: \"claude-code-glm-5\".to_string(),\n                model: None,\n                provider: None,\n                started_at: Utc::now(),\n                beads_processed: 342,\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n        registry\n            .register(crate::registry::WorkerEntry {\n                id: glm47_foxtrot.to_string(),\n                pid: std::process::id(),\n                workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n                agent: \"claude-code-glm-4_7\".to_string(),\n                model: None,\n                provider: None,\n                started_at: Utc::now(),\n                beads_processed: 0,\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Run mend as glm-4_7-foxtrot.\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new(vec![bead]);\n        let mend = MendStrand::new(\n            MendConfig::default(),\n            hb_dir.path().to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            lock_dir.path().to_path_buf(),\n            glm47_foxtrot.to_string(),\n            registry,\n            Telemetry::new(glm47_foxtrot.to_string()),\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/needle-test-logs\"),\n            0,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-traces\"),\n            30,\n            7,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-workspace\"),\n            80,\n        );\n\n        let result = mend.evaluate(&store).await;\n        assert!(\n            matches!(result, StrandResult::WorkCreated),\n            \"expected WorkCreated — bead assigned to dead glm-5-foxtrot should be orphaned, got: {result:?}\"\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed),\n            1,\n            \"bead must be released when its owner (glm-5-foxtrot) is dead\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // ── Orphaned lock file tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn orphaned_lock_file_removed() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = (store, _) = MockBeadStore::new(vec![]);\n        let mend = MendStrand::new(\n            MendConfig {\n                lock_ttl_secs: 0, // Any lock is \"old\"\n                ..MendConfig::default()\n            },\n            hb_dir.path().to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            lock_dir.path().to_path_buf(),\n            \"test-worker\".to_string(),\n            Registry::new(reg_dir.path()),\n            Telemetry::new(\"test-worker\".to_string()),\n            hb_dir.path().to_path_buf(),\n            0,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-traces\"),\n            30,\n            7,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-workspace\"),\n            80,\n        );\n\n        let result = async fn multiple_cleanups_combined() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Stale heartbeat with dead PID.\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir.path(),\n            &make_stale_heartbeat(\"dead-worker\", 99_999_999, Some(\"nd-orphan\")),\n        );\n\n        // Stale dependency link.\n        let bead = make_bead_with_deps(\n            \"blocked-bead\",\n            BeadStatus::Open,\n            vec![make_dep(\"done-blocker\", \"closed\", \"blocks\")],\n        );\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new(vec![bead]);\n        let mend = make_mend_strand(hb_dir.path(), `days_ago` days in the past.\n    fn set_mtime_days_ago(path: &Path, days_ago: u64) {\n        let past = std::time::SystemTime::now() - std::time::Duration::from_secs(days_ago * 86400);\n        filetime::set_file_mtime(path, filetime::FileTime::from_system_time(past)).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    fn make_mend_strand_with_logs(\n        hb_dir: &Path,\n        lock_dir: &Path,\n        reg_dir: &Path,\n        log_dir: &Path,\n        retention_days: u32,\n    ) -> MendStrand {\n        MendStrand::new(\n            MendConfig::default(),\n            hb_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            lock_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            \"test-worker\".to_string(),\n            Registry::new(reg_dir),\n            Telemetry::new(\"test-worker\".to_string()),\n            log_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            retention_days,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-traces\"),\n            30,\n            7,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-workspace\"),\n            80,\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn agent_log_cleanup_deletes_old_file() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let log_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Create a stale agent log (2 days old, retention = 1 day).\n        let log_path = log_dir.path().join(\"worker-abc-nd-bead1.agent.jsonl\");\n        std::fs::write(&log_path, b\"{}\").unwrap();\n        set_mtime_days_ago(&log_path, 2);\n\n        let (store, _) = MockBeadStore::new(vec![]);\n        let mend = make_mend_strand_with_logs(\n            hb_dir.path(),\n            lock_dir.path(),\n            reg_dir.path(),\n            log_dir.path(),\n            1,\n        );\n\n        let result = mend.evaluate(&store).await;\n        assert!(\n            matches!(result, StrandResult::WorkCreated),\n            \"expected WorkCreated after cleaning old agent log, got: {result:?}\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            !log_path.exists(),\n            \"stale agent log should have been deleted\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn agent_log_cleanup_skips_recent_file() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let log_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Create a fresh agent log (0 reg_dir.path(),\n            log_dir.path(),\n            1,\n        );\n\n        let result = mend.evaluate(&store).await;\n        assert!(\n            matches!(result, StrandResult::NoWork),\n agent_log_cleanup_skips_in_progress_bead() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let log_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Create an old log for a bead that is still in-progress.\n        let active_bead_id = \"nd-active1\";\n        let log_path = log_dir\n            .path()\n            .join(format!(\"worker-abc-{active_bead_id}.agent.jsonl\"));\n        std::fs::write(&log_path, b\"{}\").unwrap();\n mend.evaluate(&store).await;\n        // The in-progress bead is also \"orphaned\" by our mock (some-worker isn't\n        // registered), so mend will release it. The log must survive though.\n        assert!(\n            log_path.exists(),\n            \"agent log for in-progress bead must not be deleted\"\n        );\n        let _ = result; // outcome depends on whether orphan cleanup fires\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn agent_log_cleanup_disabled_when_retention_zero() {\n        let hb_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let lock_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let log_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Old file, but retention_days = 0 means cleanup is disabled.\n        let log_path = log_dir.path().join(\"worker-abc-nd-old.agent.jsonl\");\n        std::fs::write(&log_path, b\"{}\").unwrap();\n        set_mtime_days_ago(&log_path,
