{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_78ff9dd11bd744a790a2c678","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Atomic bead claiming with per-workspace flock serialization.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! The Claimer wraps `BeadStore.claim()` with coordination that prevents\n4\t//! thundering herd. A per-workspace flock serializes claim operations so\n5\t//! workers take turns rather than racing on the same bead.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! Depends on: `types`, `bead_store`, `telemetry`.\n8\t\n9\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n10\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n11\tuse std::io;\n12\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n13\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n14\tuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n15\t\n16\tuse anyhow::{anyhow, Result};\n17\tuse fs2::FileExt;\n18\t\n19\tuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\n20\tuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\n21\tuse crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, BeadStatus, ClaimOutcome, ClaimResult};\n22\t\n23\t/// Flock timeout: maximum time to wait for the workspace lock.\n24\tconst FLOCK_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_secs(10);\n25\t\n26\t/// Flock poll interval: time between lock acquisition attempts.\n27\tconst FLOCK_POLL_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_millis(50);\n28\t\n29\t/// Atomic bead claimer with workspace-level flock serialization.\n30\tpub struct Claimer {\n31\t    store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n32\t    lock_dir: PathBuf,\n33\t    max_retries: u32,\n34\t    retry_backoff_ms: u64,\n35\t    telemetry: Telemetry,\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\timpl Claimer {\n39\t    /// Create a new Claimer.\n40\t    ///\n41\t    /// - `store`: bead store for verify + claim operations\n42\t    /// - `lock_dir`: directory for flock files (default: `/tmp`)\n43\t    /// - `max_retries`: maximum claim attempts before giving up (default: 5)\n44\t    /// - `retry_backoff_ms`: base backoff between retries in ms (default: 100)\n45\t    /// - `telemetry`: telemetry emitter\n46\t    pub fn new(\n47\t        store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n48\t        lock_dir: PathBuf,\n49\t        max_retries: u32,\n50\t        retry_backoff_ms: u64,\n51\t        telemetry: Telemetry,\n52\t    ) -> Self {\n53\t        Claimer {\n54\t            store,\n55\t            lock_dir,\n56\t            max_retries,\n57\t            retry_backoff_ms,\n58\t            telemetry,\n59\t        }\n60\t    }\n61\t\n62\t    /// Attempt to claim the next available bead from the candidate list.\n63\t    ///\n64\t    /// Iterates candidates in priority order, skipping those in the exclusion\n65\t    /// set. For each candidate, acquires a per-workspace flock, verifies the\n66\t    /// bead is still claimable, and attempts the claim.\n67\t    ///\n68\t    /// Returns:\n69\t    /// - `Claimed(bead)`: successfully claimed a bead\n70\t    /// - `AllRaceLost`: tried candidates, all race-lost\n71\t    /// - `NoCandidates`: no candidates after filtering exclusions\n72\t    /// - `StoreError(e)`: bead store or flock error\n73\t    pub async fn claim_next(\n74\t        &self,\n75\t        candidates: &[Bead],\n76\t        actor: &str,\n77\t        exclusions: &HashSet<BeadId>,\n78\t    ) -> Result<ClaimOutcome> {\n79\t        let eligible: Vec<&Bead> = candidates\n80\t            .iter()\n81\t            .filter(|b| !exclusions.contains(&b.id))\n82\t            .collect();\n83\t\n84\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n85\t            return Ok(ClaimOutcome::NoCandidates);\n86\t        }\n87\t\n88\t        let mut attempts = 0u32;\n89\t\n90\t        for candidate in &eligible {\n91\t            if attempts >= self.max_retries {\n92\t                return Ok(ClaimOutcome::AllRaceLost);\n93\t            }\n94\t\n95\t            attempts += 1;\n96\t            let bead_id = &candidate.id;\n97\t\n98\t            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimAttempt {\n99\t                bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n100\t                attempt: attempts,\n101\t            })?;\n102\t\n103\t            // Compute workspace lock path\n104\t            let lock_path = workspace_lock_path(&self.lock_dir, &candidate.workspace);\n105\t\n106\t            // Acquire flock with timeout\n107\t            let lock_file = match acquire_flock(&lock_path).await {\n108\t                Ok(f) => f,\n109\t                Err(e) => {\n110\t                    tracing::warn!(bead_id = %bead_id, error = %e, \"flock timeout, skipping\");\n111\t                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimFailed {\n112\t                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n113\t                        reason: format!(\"flock timeout: {e}\"),\n114\t                    })?;\n115\t                    return Ok(ClaimOutcome::StoreError(e));\n116\t                }\n117\t            };\n118\t\n119\t            // Verify bead is still claimable (status=open, no assignee)\n120\t            let current = match self.store.show(bead_id).await {\n121\t                Ok(b) => b,\n122\t                Err(e) => {\n123\t                    drop(lock_file);\n124\t                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimFailed {\n125\t                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n126\t                        reason: format!(\"verify failed: {e}\"),\n127\t                    })?;\n128\t                    return Ok(ClaimOutcome::StoreError(e));\n129\t                }\n130\t            };\n131\t\n132\t            if current.status != BeadStatus::Open || current.assignee.is_some() {\n133\t                drop(lock_file);\n134\t                self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimRaceLost {\n135\t                    bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n136\t                })?;\n137\t                if attempts < self.max_retries {\n138\t                    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(\n139\t                        self.retry_backoff_ms * u64::from(attempts),\n140\t                    ))\n141\t                    .await;\n142\t                }\n143\t                continue;\n144\t            }\n145\t\n146\t            // Attempt claim via store\n147\t            let result = self.store.claim(bead_id, actor).await;\n148\t            drop(lock_file);\n149\t\n150\t            match result {\n151\t                Ok(ClaimResult::Claimed(bead)) => {\n152\t                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimSuccess {\n153\t                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n154\t                    })?;\n155\t                    return Ok(ClaimOutcome::Claimed(bead));\n156\t                }\n157\t                Ok(ClaimResult::RaceLost { .. }) => {\n158\t                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimRaceLost {\n159\t                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n160\t                    })?;\n161\t                    if attempts < self.max_retries {\n162\t                        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(\n163\t                            self.retry_backoff_ms * u64::from(attempts),\n164\t                        ))\n165\t                        .await;\n166\t                    }\n167\t                    continue;\n168\t                }\n169\t                Ok(ClaimResult::NotClaimable { reason }) => {\n170\t                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimFailed {\n171\t                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n172\t                        reason: reason.clone(),\n173\t                    })?;\n174\t                    continue;\n175\t                }\n176\t                Err(e) => {\n177\t                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimFailed {\n178\t                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n179\t                        reason: format!(\"store error: {e}\"),\n180\t                    })?;\n181\t                    return Ok(ClaimOutcome::StoreError(e));\n182\t                }\n183\t            }\n184\t        }\n185\t\n186\t        // Exhausted all eligible candidates without success\n187\t        Ok(ClaimOutcome::AllRaceLost)\n188\t    }\n189\t\n190\t    /// Convenience: claim a single bead by ID (fetches the bead, then delegates\n191\t    /// to `claim_next` with a single-element candidate list).\n192\t    pub async fn claim_one(&self, bead_id: &BeadId, actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n193\t        let bead = self.store.show(bead_id).await?;\n194\t        let exclusions = HashSet::new();\n195\t        match self.claim_next(&[bead], actor, &exclusions).await? {\n196\t            ClaimOutcome::Claimed(b) => Ok(ClaimResult::Claimed(b)),\n197\t            ClaimOutcome::AllRaceLost => Ok(ClaimResult::RaceLost {\n198\t                claimed_by: \"(race)\".to_string(),\n199\t            }),\n200\t            ClaimOutcome::NoCandidates => Ok(ClaimResult::NotClaimable {\n201\t                reason: \"no candidates\".to_string(),\n202\t            }),\n203\t            ClaimOutcome::StoreError(e) => Err(e),\n204\t        }\n205\t    }\n206\t}\n207\t\n208\t/// Compute a deterministic lock file path for a workspace.\n209\t///\n210\t/// Uses a simple hash (not crypto) of the workspace path. All workers\n211\t/// compute the same hash for the same workspace.\n212\tfn workspace_lock_path(lock_dir: &Path, workspace: &Path) -> PathBuf {\n213\t    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n214\t    workspace.hash(&mut hasher);\n215\t    let hash = hasher.finish();\n216\t    lock_dir.join(format!(\"needle-claim-{:016x}.lock\", hash))\n217\t}\n218\t\n219\t/// Acquire an exclusive flock with a 10-second timeout.\n220\t///\n221\t/// Returns the locked file on success. The lock is released when the\n222\t/// file is dropped (flock auto-releases on close).\n223\tpub async fn acquire_flock(lock_path: &Path) -> Result<std::fs::File> {\n224\t    if let Some(parent) = lock_path.parent() {\n225\t        std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;\n226\t    }\n227\t\n228\t    let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n229\t        .create(true)\n230\t        .truncate(false)\n231\t        .write(true)\n232\t        .open(lock_path)?;\n233\t\n234\t    let deadline = Instant::now() + FLOCK_TIMEOUT;\n235\t    loop {\n236\t        match file.try_lock_exclusive() {\n237\t            Ok(()) => return Ok(file),\n238\t            Err(ref e) if e.kind() == io::ErrorKind::WouldBlock => {\n239\t                if Instant::now() >= deadline {\n240\t                    return Err(anyhow!(\n241\t                        \"flock timeout after {}s on {}\",\n242\t                        FLOCK_TIMEOUT.as_secs(),\n243\t                        lock_path.display()\n244\t                    ));\n245\t                }\n246\t                tokio::time::sleep(FLOCK_POLL_INTERVAL).await;\n247\t            }\n248\t            Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),\n249\t        }\n250\t    }\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n254\t\n255\t#[cfg(test)]\n256\tmod tests {\n257\t   .unwrap();\n577\t        assert!(matches!(result, ClaimOutcome::NoCandidates));\n578\t    }\n579\t\n580\t    #[tokio::test]\n581\t    async fn max_retries_caps_attempts() {\n582\t        // Create         .collect();\n591\t        let store = Arc::new(MockBeadStore::new(beads.clone()).with_claim_results(results));\n592\t        // max_retries = 3 — should stop after 3 attempts\n593\t        let .await\n604\t            .unwrap();\n605\t        assert!(matches!(result, ClaimOutcome::AllRaceLost));\n606\t    }\n607\t}\n608\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8d2ee6f9-9054-4e1b-b4ba-04f7ef472c61","uuid":"8b273413-3246-482c-a57e-755cfd04f9de","timestamp":"2026-04-24T12:33:37.844Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/claim/mod.rs","content":"//! Atomic bead claiming with per-workspace flock serialization.\n//!\n//! The Claimer wraps `BeadStore.claim()` with coordination that prevents\n//! thundering herd. A per-workspace flock serializes claim operations so\n//! workers take turns rather than racing on the same bead.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `types`, `bead_store`, `telemetry`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n\nuse anyhow::{anyhow, Result};\nuse fs2::FileExt;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, BeadStatus, ClaimOutcome, ClaimResult};\n\n/// Flock timeout: maximum time to wait for the workspace lock.\nconst FLOCK_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_secs(10);\n\n/// Flock poll interval: time between lock acquisition attempts.\nconst FLOCK_POLL_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_millis(50);\n\n/// Atomic bead claimer with workspace-level flock serialization.\npub struct Claimer {\n    store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n    lock_dir: PathBuf,\n    max_retries: u32,\n    retry_backoff_ms: u64,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n}\n\nimpl Claimer {\n    /// Create a new Claimer.\n    ///\n    /// - `store`: bead store for verify + claim operations\n    /// - `lock_dir`: directory for flock files (default: `/tmp`)\n    /// - `max_retries`: maximum claim attempts before giving up (default: 5)\n    /// - `retry_backoff_ms`: base backoff between retries in ms (default: 100)\n    /// - `telemetry`: telemetry emitter\n    pub fn new(\n        store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n        lock_dir: PathBuf,\n        max_retries: u32,\n        retry_backoff_ms: u64,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Claimer {\n            store,\n            lock_dir,\n            max_retries,\n            retry_backoff_ms,\n            telemetry,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Attempt to claim the next available bead from the candidate list.\n    ///\n    /// Iterates candidates in priority order, skipping those in the exclusion\n    /// set. For each candidate, acquires a per-workspace flock, verifies the\n    /// bead is still claimable, and attempts the claim.\n    ///\n    /// Returns:\n    /// - `Claimed(bead)`: successfully claimed a bead\n    /// - `AllRaceLost`: tried candidates, all race-lost\n    /// - `NoCandidates`: no candidates after filtering exclusions\n    /// - `StoreError(e)`: bead store or flock error\n    pub async fn claim_next(\n        &self,\n        candidates: &[Bead],\n        actor: &str,\n        exclusions: &HashSet<BeadId>,\n    ) -> Result<ClaimOutcome> {\n        let eligible: Vec<&Bead> = candidates\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|b| !exclusions.contains(&b.id))\n            .collect();\n\n        if eligible.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(ClaimOutcome::NoCandidates);\n        }\n\n        let mut attempts = 0u32;\n\n        for candidate in &eligible {\n            if attempts >= self.max_retries {\n                return Ok(ClaimOutcome::AllRaceLost);\n            }\n\n            attempts += 1;\n            let bead_id = &candidate.id;\n\n            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimAttempt {\n                bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                attempt: attempts,\n            })?;\n\n            // Compute workspace lock path\n            let lock_path = workspace_lock_path(&self.lock_dir, &candidate.workspace);\n\n            // Acquire flock with timeout\n            let lock_file = match acquire_flock(&lock_path).await {\n                Ok(f) => f,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(bead_id = %bead_id, error = %e, \"flock timeout, skipping\");\n                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimFailed {\n                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                        reason: format!(\"flock timeout: {e}\"),\n                    })?;\n                    return Ok(ClaimOutcome::StoreError(e));\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Verify bead is still claimable (status=open, no assignee)\n            let current = match self.store.show(bead_id).await {\n                Ok(b) => b,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    drop(lock_file);\n                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimFailed {\n                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                        reason: format!(\"verify failed: {e}\"),\n                    })?;\n                    return Ok(ClaimOutcome::StoreError(e));\n                }\n            };\n\n            if current.status != BeadStatus::Open || current.assignee.is_some() {\n                drop(lock_file);\n                self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimRaceLost {\n                    bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                })?;\n                if attempts < self.max_retries {\n                    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(\n                        self.retry_backoff_ms * u64::from(attempts),\n                    ))\n                    .await;\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Attempt claim via store\n            let result = self.store.claim(bead_id, actor).await;\n            drop(lock_file);\n\n            match result {\n                Ok(ClaimResult::Claimed(bead)) => {\n                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimSuccess {\n                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                    })?;\n                    return Ok(ClaimOutcome::Claimed(bead));\n                }\n                Ok(ClaimResult::RaceLost { .. }) => {\n                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimRaceLost {\n                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                    })?;\n                    if attempts < self.max_retries {\n                        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(\n                            self.retry_backoff_ms * u64::from(attempts),\n                        ))\n                        .await;\n                    }\n                    continue;\n                }\n                Ok(ClaimResult::NotClaimable { reason }) => {\n                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimFailed {\n                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                        reason: reason.clone(),\n                    })?;\n                    continue;\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimFailed {\n                        bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                        reason: format!(\"store error: {e}\"),\n                    })?;\n                    return Ok(ClaimOutcome::StoreError(e));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Exhausted all eligible candidates without success\n        Ok(ClaimOutcome::AllRaceLost)\n    }\n\n    /// Convenience: claim a single bead by ID (fetches the bead, then delegates\n    /// to `claim_next` with a single-element candidate list).\n    pub async fn claim_one(&self, bead_id: &BeadId, actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n        let bead = self.store.show(bead_id).await?;\n        let exclusions = HashSet::new();\n        match self.claim_next(&[bead], actor, &exclusions).await? {\n            ClaimOutcome::Claimed(b) => Ok(ClaimResult::Claimed(b)),\n            ClaimOutcome::AllRaceLost => Ok(ClaimResult::RaceLost {\n                claimed_by: \"(race)\".to_string(),\n            }),\n            ClaimOutcome::NoCandidates => Ok(ClaimResult::NotClaimable {\n                reason: \"no candidates\".to_string(),\n            }),\n            ClaimOutcome::StoreError(e) => Err(e),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compute a deterministic lock file path for a workspace.\n///\n/// Uses a simple hash (not crypto) of the workspace path. All workers\n/// compute the same hash for the same workspace.\nfn workspace_lock_path(lock_dir: &Path, workspace: &Path) -> PathBuf {\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    workspace.hash(&mut hasher);\n    let hash = hasher.finish();\n    lock_dir.join(format!(\"needle-claim-{:016x}.lock\", hash))\n}\n\n/// Acquire an exclusive flock with a 10-second timeout.\n///\n/// Returns the locked file on success. The lock is released when the\n/// file is dropped (flock auto-releases on close).\npub async fn acquire_flock(lock_path: &Path) -> Result<std::fs::File> {\n    if let Some(parent) = lock_path.parent() {\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;\n    }\n\n    let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n        .create(true)\n        .truncate(false)\n        .write(true)\n        .open(lock_path)?;\n\n    let deadline = Instant::now() + FLOCK_TIMEOUT;\n    loop {\n        match file.try_lock_exclusive() {\n            Ok(()) => return Ok(file),\n            Err(ref e) if e.kind() == io::ErrorKind::WouldBlock => {\n                if Instant::now() >= deadline {\n                    return Err(anyhow!(\n                        \"flock timeout after {}s on {}\",\n                        FLOCK_TIMEOUT.as_secs(),\n                        lock_path.display()\n                    ));\n                }\n                tokio::time::sleep(FLOCK_POLL_INTERVAL).await;\n            }\n            Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters, RepairReport};\n    use crate::telemetry::Telemetry;\n    use crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, BeadStatus, ClaimResult};\n    use async_trait::async_trait;\n    use std::sync::Mutex;\n\n    fn make_bead(id: &str, workspace: &str) -> Bead {\n        Bead {\n            id: BeadId::from(id),\n            title: format!(\"Test bead {id}\"),\n            body: None,\n            priority: 1,\n            status: BeadStatus::Open,\n            assignee: None,\n            labels: vec![],\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(workspace),\n            dependencies: vec![],\n            dependents: vec![],\n            created_at: chrono::Utc::now(),\n            updated_at: chrono::Utc::now(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Mock bead store that returns configurable claim results.\n    struct MockBeadStore {\n        beads: Mutex<Vec<Bead>>,\n        /// Claim results consumed in FIFO order; when empty, claims succeed.\n        claim_results: Mutex<Vec<ClaimResult>>,\n    }\n\n    impl MockBeadStore {\n        fn new(beads: Vec<Bead>) -> Self {\n            MockBeadStore {\n                beads: Mutex::new(beads),\n                claim_results: Mutex::new(vec![]),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn with_claim_results(self, results: Vec<ClaimResult>) -> Self {\n            *self.claim_results.lock().unwrap() = results;\n            self\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for MockBeadStore {\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(self.beads.lock().unwrap().clone())\n        }\n        async fn ready(&self, _filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(self.beads.lock().unwrap().clone())\n        }\n\n        async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            self.beads\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .iter()\n                .find(|b| b.id == *id)\n                .cloned()\n                .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"bead not found: {id}\"))\n        }\n\n        async fn claim(&self, id: &BeadId, actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            {\n                let mut results = self.claim_results.lock().unwrap();\n                if !results.is_empty() {\n                    return Ok(results.remove(0));\n                }\n            }\n            // Default: claim succeeds\n            let mut bead = self\n                .beads\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .iter()\n                .find(|b| b.id == *id)\n                .cloned()\n                .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"bead not found: {id}\"))?;\n            bead.status = BeadStatus::InProgress;\n            bead.assignee = Some(actor.to_string());\n            Ok(ClaimResult::Claimed(bead))\n        }\n\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn labels(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn remove_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"new-bead\".to_string()))\n        }\n\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn add_dependency(&self, _blocker_id: &BeadId, _blocked_id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_claimer(store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>) -> Claimer {\n        Claimer::new(\n            store,\n            std::env::temp_dir(),\n            5,\n            10, // short backoff for tests\n            Telemetry::new(\"test-worker\".to_string()),\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn claim_next_empty_candidates_returns_no_candidates() {\n        let store = Arc::new(MockBeadStore::new(vec![]));\n        let claimer = make_claimer(store);\n        let result = claimer\n            .claim_next(&[], \"worker-1\", &HashSet::new())\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n        assert!(matches!(result, ClaimOutcome::NoCandidates));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn claim_next_all_excluded_returns_no_candidates() {\n        let bead = make_bead(\"needle-abc\", \"/tmp/ws\");\n        let store = Arc::new(MockBeadStore::new(vec![bead.clone()]));\n        let claimer = make_claimer(store);\n        let mut exclusions = HashSet::new();\n        exclusions.insert(BeadId::from(\"needle-abc\"));\n\n        let result = claimer\n            .claim_next(&[bead], \"worker-1\", &exclusions)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n        assert!(matches!(result, ClaimOutcome::NoCandidates));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn claim_next_happy_path_returns_claimed() {\n        let bead = make_bead(\"needle-abc\", \"/tmp/ws\");\n        let store = Arc::new(MockBeadStore::new(vec![bead.clone()]));\n        let claimer = make_claimer(store);\n\n        let result = claimer\n            .claim_next(&[bead], \"worker-1\", &HashSet::new())\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n        match result {\n Pluck strand: primary bead selection from the assigned workspace.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Pluck handles >90% of all bead processing. It queries the bead store for\n4\t//! unassigned, ready beads, filters by excluded labels, and sorts them in\n5\t//! deterministic priority order: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same candidate list.\n8\t\n9\tuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters};\n10\tuse crate::types::{Bead, StrandError, StrandResult};\n11\t\n12\t/// Default labels excluded from Pluck selection when not configured.\n13\tconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n14\t\n15\t/// The Pluck strand — primary work selection.\n16\tpub struct PluckStrand {\n17\t    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n18\t    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n19\t}\n20\t\n21\timpl PluckStrand {\n22\t    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels.\n23\t    ///\n24\t    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n25\t    /// `blocked`) is used.\n26\t    pub fn new(exclude_labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n27\t        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n28\t            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n29\t                .iter()\n30\t                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n31\t                .collect()\n32\t        } else {\n33\t            exclude_labels\n34\t        };\n35\t        PluckStrand {\n36\t            exclude_labels: labels,\n37\t        }\n38\t    }\n39\t\n40\t    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n41\t    ///\n42\t    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n43\t    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n44\t    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n45\t        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n46\t            a.priority\n47\t                .cmp(&b.priority)\n48\t                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n49\t                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n50\t        });\n51\t    }\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n55\timpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n56\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n57\t        \"pluck\"\n58\t    }\n59\t\n60\t    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n61\t        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n62\t        let filters = Filters {\n63\t            assignee: None,\n64\t            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n65\t        };\n66\t\n67\t        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n68\t            Ok(beads) => beads,\n69\t            Err(e) => {\n70\t                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n71\t                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n72\t            }\n73\t        };\n74\t\n75\t        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n76\t        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n77\t        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n78\t        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n79\t        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n80\t        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n81\t        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n82\t        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n83\t\n84\t        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker).\n85\t        //    br ready returns open/claimable beads, but an open bead may have a stale\n86\t        //    assignee string from a previous claim that was released. We only exclude\n87\t        //    beads whose status is in_progress — not beads with a leftover assignee field.\n88\t        candidates.retain(|b| !matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress));\n89\t\n90\t        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n91\t        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n92\t\n93\t        // 5. Return result.\n94\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n95\t            StrandResult::NoWork\n96\t        } else {\n97\t            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n98\t        }\n99\t Pluck strand: primary bead selection from the assigned workspace.\n//!\n//! Pluck handles >90% of all bead processing. It queries the bead store for\n//! unassigned, ready beads, filters by excluded labels, and sorts them in\n//! deterministic priority order: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n//!\n//! Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same candidate list.\n\nuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters};\nuse crate::types::{Bead, StrandError, StrandResult};\n\n/// Default labels excluded from Pluck selection when not configured.\nconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n\n/// The Pluck strand — primary work selection.\npub struct PluckStrand {\n    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n}\n\nimpl PluckStrand {\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn new(exclude_labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n    ///\n    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n            a.priority\n                .cmp(&b.priority)\n                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n        });\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"pluck\"\n    }\n\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n        let filters = Filters {\n            assignee: None,\n            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n        };\n\n        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n            Ok(beads) => beads,\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n            }\n        };\n\n        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n\n        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker).\n        //    br ready returns open/claimable beads, but an open bead may have a stale\n        //    assignee string from a previous claim that was released. We only exclude\n        //    beads whose status is in_progress — not beads with a leftover assignee field.\n        candidates.retain(|b| !matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress));\n\n        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n\n        // 5. Return result.\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        } else {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::RepairReport;\n    use crate::types::{BeadId, BeadStatus, ClaimResult};\n\n    use anyhow::Result;\n    use chrono::{TimeZone, Utc};\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\n\n    /// In-memory bead store for testing.\n    struct MemoryStore {\n        beads: Vec<Bead>,\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for MemoryStore {\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(self.beads.clone())\n        }\n        async fn ready(&self, filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            let result: Vec<Bead> = self\n                .beads\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|b| {\n                    // Filter by assignee if set.\n                    if let Some(ref a) = filters.assignee {\n                        if b.assignee.as_ref() != Some(a) {\n                            return false;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Filter out beads with excluded labels.\n                    if b.labels.iter().any(|l| filters.exclude_labels.contains(l)) {\n                        return false;\n                    }\n                    true\n                })\n                .cloned()\n                .collect();\n            Ok(result)\n        }\n\n        async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            self.beads\n                .iter()\n                .find(|b| b.id == *id)\n                .cloned()\n                .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!(\"bead not found: {id}\"))\n        }\n\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented\")\n        }\n\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn labels(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            let bead = self.show(id).await?;\n            Ok(bead.labels)\n        }\n\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn remove_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"new-bead\".to_string()))\n        }\n\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n   }\n\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"store connection failed\")\n        }\n\n        async fn let dt = chrono::NaiveDateTime::parse_from_str(created_at, \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n            .expect(\"bad test date\");\n        Bead {\n            id: BeadId::from(id.to_string()),\n            title: format!(\"Bead {id}\"),\n            body: None,\n            priority,\n            status: BeadStatus::Open,\n            assignee: None,\n            labels: vec![],\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            dependencies: vec![],\n            dependents: vec![],\n            created_at: Utc.from_utc_datetime(&dt),\n            updated_at: Utc.from_utc_datetime(&dt),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_bead_with_labels(id: &str, priority: u8, labels: Vec<&str>) -> Bead {\n        let mut bead = make_bead(id, priority, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\");\n        bead.labels = labels.into_iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect();\n        bead\n    }\n\n    fn make_bead_with_assignee(id: &str, assignee: &str) -> Bead {\n        let mut bead = make_bead(id, 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\");\n        bead.assignee = Some(assignee.to_string());\n        bead\n    }\n\n    use super::super::Strand;\n\n    // NoWork causes the worker\n        // to move to Exhausted rather than spinning the claim-retry loop.\n        let store = UnfilteredStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-1\", 1, vec![\"deferred\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-2\", 2, vec![\"deferred\", \"human\"]),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::NoWork => {}\n            other => panic!(\"expected NoWork, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn open_bead_with_stale_assignee_is_claimable() {\n        // An open bead with a leftover assignee from a previous claim is still\n        // claimable.
